profile.access_model: Restricted to authorized US government and military personnel. Classified and unclassified versions. No public access. Access via DoD network credentials and need-to-know basis.

L1_Surveillance.d: University of Sydney AI-powered open-source epidemic observatory

profile.overview: EPIWATCH is an AI-powered open-source epidemic observatory developed by the University of Sydney's Biosecurity Program, led by Professor Raina MacIntyre. Uses AI and machine learning for early detection of emerging disease outbreaks globally.
profile.functional_scope: Real-time AI-driven epidemic intelligence scanning open-source data (news, social media, official reports) in multiple languages. Automated anomaly detection, disease event classification, outbreak early warning, and risk assessment. Focused on pandemic preparedness and biosecurity.
profile.tech_stack: Machine learning and NLP classifiers for disease event extraction. Web scraping and data aggregation pipelines. Interactive web dashboard. Cloud-hosted analytics platform.
profile.operator: University of Sydney, Kirby Institute/Biosecurity Program, Sydney, Australia. Led by Professor Raina MacIntyre. Funded by Australian government and research grants.
profile.data_model: Structured disease event records from unstructured sources. Each event tagged with pathogen, location, date, severity, and source. Time series of outbreak signals with geolocation. Exportable alert feeds.
profile.users_scale: Used by Australian health authorities, WHO, and international public health partners. Thousands of users accessing the public dashboard. Active research output with regular publications.
profile.access_model: Open-source platform with free public access to alerts and dashboards at epiwatch.org. API access for institutional partners. Enhanced analytics for government subscribers.

L1_Surveillance.d: FAO/WHO/WOAH Global Early Warning System for health threats at the human-animal-environment interface

profile.overview: GLEWS+ (Global Early Warning System) is a joint FAO/WHO/WOAH (OIE) initiative for early detection and assessment of health threats at the human-animal-environment interface, established as part of the One Health Tripartite collaboration.
profile.functional_scope: Early warning for zoonotic diseases and other health threats at the human-animal-ecosystem interface. Risk assessment, event verification, joint rapid risk analysis, and coordinated response across the three agencies. Monitors emerging diseases, AMR, food safety.
profile.tech_stack: Event-based surveillance integrating FAO EMPRES-i, WHO Disease Outbreak News, WOAH WAHIS data streams. Risk assessment frameworks, joint analysis protocols, GIS mapping.
profile.operator: FAO (Rome), WHO (Geneva), WOAH (Paris) - Tripartite One Health partnership. Supported by international donor governments.
profile.data_model: Multi-source event data: animal disease outbreaks, human disease alerts, environmental signals. Structured joint risk assessment reports. GIS-referenced event records.
profile.users_scale: Serves 194 WHO member states and FAO/WOAH member countries. Used by national veterinary and public health authorities worldwide. Core One Health surveillance coordination.
profile.access_model: Intergovernmental system. Published risk assessments available via FAO/WHO/WOAH portals. Alert feed access for national focal points. Technical reports freely downloadable.

L1_Surveillance.d: WHO's Global Antimicrobial Resistance and Use Surveillance System, collecting standardized AMR data from national surveillance systems in 120+ countries

profile.overview: GLASS (Global Antimicrobial Resistance and Use Surveillance System) is WHO's comprehensive global surveillance initiative for antimicrobial resistance. Launched in October 2015, it provides the first worldwide standardized approach to collecting, analyzing, and sharing AMR and antimicrobial consumption data.
profile.functional_scope: Multiple surveillance modules: GLASS-AMR (routine AMR data from clinical samples), GLASS-AMC (antimicrobial consumption monitoring), GLASS-EAR (emerging resistance reporting), GLASS-FUNGI (invasive fungal infections), EGASP (gonococcal resistance surveillance), One Health (ESBL-E. coli Tricycle project), PPS-AMU (hospital antibiotic use surveys), and AMR Burden estimation studies.
profile.tech_stack: WHO GLASS platform with data submission portals, WHONET integration for data collection, R/Shiny interactive dashboards for visualization, custom analytical frameworks. Standardized data submission formats.
profile.operator: World Health Organization (WHO), Department of Surveillance, Prevention and Control of AMR. Supported by WHO AMR Surveillance Collaborating Centres Network.
profile.data_model: Standardized AMR data: pathogen-antibiotic combinations, resistance rates by specimen type. Epidemiological metadata (country, year, setting). AMC data in DDD (Defined Daily Doses) per 1,000 inhabitants.
profile.users_scale: 127 countries enrolled. Published annual reports since 2017 informing global AMR policy. Data from thousands of laboratories worldwide.
profile.access_model: Free enrollment for WHO Member States. Data submission through GLASS portal. Public dashboards and annual reports freely available. Country-level data published with consent.

L1_Surveillance.d: CDC national arboviral surveillance system tracking human, animal, and mosquito data

profile.overview: ArboNET is CDC's national arboviral surveillance system tracking human disease cases and animal/mosquito infections for West Nile virus, Zika, dengue, chikungunya, EEE, and other arboviruses in the United States.
profile.functional_scope: Case-based surveillance for arboviral diseases: human neuroinvasive and non-neuroinvasive cases, veterinary cases, dead bird surveillance, mosquito surveillance. Collects clinical, epidemiological, and laboratory data. Annual and provisional reporting.
profile.tech_stack: Web-based case reporting through state/territorial health departments. Integrated with CDC NNDSS for notifiable conditions. CDC WONDER for data access. Standard CDC surveillance architecture.
profile.operator: CDC Division of Vector-Borne Diseases (DVBD), Fort Collins, CO, USA. All state, territorial, and local health departments report.
profile.data_model: Case-level data: demographics, symptoms, lab results (IgM, PRNT), exposure history, travel. Animal/mosquito surveillance data with collection site, date, species. Annual summary statistics.
profile.users_scale: Covers all US states and territories. Tracks tens of thousands of arboviral cases annually. Data used by state/local health departments, vector control programs, and researchers.
profile.access_model: Aggregate data publicly available via CDC website and CDC WONDER. Case-level data restricted to authorized personnel. Annual summaries freely published.

L1_Surveillance.d: CDC's Data Modernization Initiative for unified public health data infrastructure

profile.overview: CDC's Data Modernization Initiative (DMI) aims to transform public health data systems into a unified, cloud-based, real-time infrastructure. The 'One CDC' data platform concept unifies siloed surveillance systems.
profile.functional_scope: Modernizing US public health data infrastructure: cloud migration, API-based data exchange, FHIR interoperability, automated case reporting, real-time dashboards, AI/ML analytics, and electronic case reporting (eCR). Replacing legacy systems.
profile.tech_stack: Cloud-first architecture (Azure/AWS). FHIR R4 standard, HL7v2 messaging, API gateways. ReportStream for data routing. DCIPHER data platform. Snowflake data warehouse. Tableau dashboards.
profile.operator: CDC Office of Public Health Data, Surveillance, and Technology (OPHDST), Atlanta, GA. $200M+ annual investment authorized by Congress.
profile.data_model: Unified data model spanning multiple surveillance domains. FHIR-based electronic case reports. Standardized message formats. Cloud data lakes with analytics layers.
profile.users_scale: Serves all US state, tribal, local, and territorial health departments. Impacts 330M population. Thousands of public health professionals and data systems connected.
profile.access_model: Government infrastructure program. Systems accessible to authorized public health officials. Public APIs for standardized data exchange. Open-source components (ReportStream).

L1_Surveillance.n: Google Earth Engine / Health Map AI
L1_Surveillance.d: Google Earth Engine combined with AI models for environmental and epidemiological surveillance - used by WHO/FAO for vector-borne disease risk mapping and climate-health forecasting

profile.overview: Google Earth Engine (GEE) is a cloud-based geospatial analysis platform combined with AI/ML models for environmental and disease ecology applications. Provides petabyte-scale satellite imagery analysis for health-related geospatial intelligence.
profile.functional_scope: Environmental determinants of disease: land use change, deforestation tracking, climate variables, water body detection, urbanization monitoring. Disease ecology modeling, mosquito habitat mapping, pollution monitoring, and climate-health associations.
profile.tech_stack: Google Cloud infrastructure. JavaScript/Python API. Petabytes of satellite imagery (Landsat, Sentinel, MODIS). TensorFlow integration for ML models. Earth Engine Code Editor.
profile.operator: Google LLC, Mountain View, CA, USA. Free for research and nonprofit use. Earth Engine for Health partnerships with WHO and academic institutions.
profile.data_model: Raster satellite imagery collections with temporal metadata. Vector datasets (boundaries, infrastructure). User-uploaded assets. Computed raster/vector outputs. Time series exports.
profile.users_scale: Millions of registered users. Used by WHO, FAO, World Bank, NASA, and thousands of research institutions. 100,000+ published studies. Key tool for environmental health research.
profile.access_model: Free for research, education, and nonprofit use. Commercial licensing via Google Cloud. Browser-based Code Editor. Python/JavaScript API. Google account required.

L1_Surveillance.d: Centers of Excellence for Influenza Research and Response / Influenza Data Processing Coordination Center

profile.overview: The Centers of Excellence for Influenza Research and Response (CEIRR), succeeding CEIRS, is an NIAID-funded network of multidisciplinary research centers focused on influenza and other respiratory pathogens, including the Influenza Data Processing and Coordination Center (iDPCC).
profile.functional_scope: Influenza and respiratory virus research: surveillance, virus characterization, vaccine development, antiviral research, animal models, computational analysis. iDPCC provides centralized data management and bioinformatics support.
profile.tech_stack: Genomic sequencing (Illumina, Nanopore), antigenic characterization, structural biology, computational modeling. iDPCC data platform with analysis tools. FASTA/GenBank submissions.
profile.operator: NIAID/NIH-funded network. Multiple US academic centers (St. Jude, Emory, Icahn Mt Sinai, Scripps, U. Rochester). iDPCC at Northrop Grumman/academic partners.
profile.data_model: Influenza viral sequences, antigenic data, surveillance metadata, animal model results. Centralized in iDPCC. Submissions to GISAID and NCBI GenBank.
profile.users_scale: Five major research centers plus associates. Generates thousands of viral sequences annually. Data shared globally through GISAID. Informs WHO vaccine strain selection.
profile.access_model: NIAID-funded research program. Data shared publicly through GISAID/GenBank. Publications in open literature. iDPCC provides tools and analysis to network members.

L1_Surveillance.d: Saudi Arabia Health Electronic Surveillance Network for notifiable diseases and Hajj monitoring

profile.overview: The Health Electronic Surveillance Network (HESN) is Saudi Arabia's national digital disease surveillance system operated by the Ministry of Health for real-time monitoring of notifiable diseases and health events.
profile.functional_scope: National electronic disease surveillance: notifiable disease reporting, real-time case tracking, outbreak detection and response, Hajj/Umrah mass gathering surveillance, syndromic surveillance, and AMR monitoring across all Saudi healthcare facilities.
profile.tech_stack: Web-based electronic reporting system. Integrated with hospital information systems. GIS mapping. Real-time dashboards. Mobile reporting applications.
profile.operator: Saudi Arabia Ministry of Health (MOH), Riyadh, Saudi Arabia. Mandatory reporting from all public and private healthcare facilities.
profile.data_model: Case-level surveillance data for notifiable diseases. Syndromic surveillance signals. Laboratory results. Demographic and geographic data. Mass gathering health monitoring.
profile.users_scale: Covers all Saudi Arabian healthcare facilities and population (~36M). Critical for Hajj surveillance (2-3M pilgrims annually). Reports to WHO IHR focal point.
profile.access_model: Government surveillance system. Accessible to authorized MOH personnel and healthcare facilities. Aggregate data shared with WHO. Not publicly accessible.

L1_Surveillance.d: ROK-US syndromic surveillance system for joint biosurveillance on Korean peninsula including Able Response exercises and KDCA integration

profile.overview: The ROK-US Joint Biosurveillance Portal is a bilateral syndromic surveillance system operated by South Korean KDCA and US Forces Korea for joint monitoring of infectious disease threats on the Korean Peninsula.
profile.functional_scope: Joint syndromic surveillance between ROK and USFK: real-time disease monitoring in military and civilian populations, data sharing for outbreak detection, coordinated response planning, and bi-national health threat assessment.
profile.tech_stack: Web-based surveillance portal. Electronic health record integration from military and civilian health systems. Syndromic surveillance algorithms. Bilingual interface (Korean/English).
profile.operator: Korea Disease Control and Prevention Agency (KDCA) and US Forces Korea (USFK) public health command. Bilateral defense agreement framework.
profile.data_model: Syndromic surveillance data from military treatment facilities and Korean hospitals. ICD-coded encounters, chief complaint data, laboratory results. Aggregate trend analysis.
profile.users_scale: Covers USFK personnel (28,500+ troops) and surrounding Korean population. Both military and civilian health data streams. Used for peninsula-wide threat monitoring.
profile.access_model: Bilateral government/military access only. Not publicly available. Operates under ROK-US alliance health cooperation agreements.

L1_Surveillance.d: Global platform for sharing antimicrobial resistance surveillance data from pharma partners

profile.overview: Vivli AMR Register is a global platform for sharing antimicrobial resistance surveillance data, operated by Vivli (a nonprofit data sharing platform) in partnership with pharma companies and academic institutions.
profile.functional_scope: Aggregates and shares AMR surveillance data from pharmaceutical company surveillance programs (Pfizer ATLAS, MSD, etc.) and academic networks. Provides interactive dashboards, data exploration tools, and downloadable datasets for AMR research.
profile.tech_stack: Web-based data platform with interactive dashboards. RESTful API. Data anonymization and harmonization pipelines. Cloud-hosted on Vivli infrastructure.
profile.operator: Vivli Inc. (nonprofit), Cambridge, MA, USA. Data contributors include Pfizer, MSD, Shionogi, academic AMR surveillance networks.
profile.data_model: MIC (minimum inhibitory concentration) data, organism identification, geographic origin, specimen type, year. Standardized across multiple contributing surveillance programs.
profile.users_scale: Millions of AMR data records from global surveillance. Used by WHO, researchers, pharma companies, and public health agencies. Open to qualified researchers.
profile.access_model: Free access to aggregate dashboards at amr.vivli.org. Detailed data access through Vivli data request process. Researcher registration required for full datasets.

L1_Surveillance.d: Harmonized Hajj Health Information System with Health Early Warning System for Hajj/Umrah

profile.overview: Harmonized Hajj Health Information System (HHIS) with Health Early Warning System (HEWS) provides mass gathering disease surveillance during the annual Hajj pilgrimage in Saudi Arabia, monitoring 2-3 million pilgrims from 180+ countries.
profile.functional_scope: Real-time syndromic surveillance during Hajj: monitoring respiratory, gastrointestinal, and vector-borne diseases. Hospital admissions tracking, laboratory confirmation, environmental monitoring, and rapid response coordination across Hajj healthcare facilities.
profile.tech_stack: Electronic surveillance integrated with Saudi Ministry of Health systems (HESN). Mobile reporting, GIS mapping of disease clusters, laboratory information systems. Integration with WHO IHR reporting.
profile.operator: Saudi Ministry of Health (MOH) in coordination with WHO. Operational during Hajj and Umrah seasons. International health authorities collaborate.
profile.data_model: Syndromic surveillance data from Hajj healthcare facilities. Case-level records, laboratory results, environmental monitoring data. Daily situation reports. Post-Hajj analytical reports.
profile.users_scale: Covers 2-3 million annual Hajj pilgrims from 180+ countries. Hundreds of healthcare workers report. Critical for global health security given mass gathering dynamics.
profile.access_model: Government health system. Internal to Saudi MOH and WHO during Hajj operations. Post-season aggregate reports published. Not publicly accessible in real-time.

L1_Surveillance.d: Open repository of US and global disease surveillance data spanning over a century

profile.overview: Project Tycho is an open repository of US and global disease surveillance data spanning 130+ years, developed by the University of Pittsburgh Graduate School of Public Health. Named after astronomer Tycho Brahe for systematic data collection.
profile.functional_scope: Digitized historical US disease surveillance data from 1888 to present (all notifiable diseases). Global disease data from WHO and national sources. Time series of disease incidence by location. Enables analysis of vaccination impact and long-term disease trends.
profile.tech_stack: PostgreSQL database with web interface. REST API for programmatic access. R and Python packages for data retrieval. Standardized data formats (CSV, JSON).
profile.operator: University of Pittsburgh Graduate School of Public Health, Pittsburgh, PA, USA. Funded by Gates Foundation, NIH, and NSF.
profile.data_model: Weekly/monthly disease case counts by US state/city from 1888-present. Standardized disease names, geographic codes, and temporal resolution. 56M+ data points. 90+ diseases.
profile.users_scale: Cited in hundreds of publications including Science, Nature, PNAS. Used by epidemiologists, historians, policy researchers. Free access for academic use.
profile.access_model: Free public access for academic and nonprofit use at tycho.pitt.edu. Registration required. Commercial use license available. API and bulk download.

L1_Surveillance.d: Regional health surveillance coordination platform enabling multi-country data sharing and synchronized response for cross-border disease monitoring

profile.overview: SAP in the biosurveillance context refers to regional health surveillance coordination platforms enabling standardized disease reporting and cross-border health intelligence sharing.
profile.functional_scope: Regional surveillance coordination: standardized case reporting formats, cross-border disease intelligence sharing, harmonized laboratory data, and coordinated outbreak response between participating health systems.
profile.tech_stack: Web-based reporting platform. HL7/FHIR interoperability. Dashboard visualization. Secure data exchange protocols.
profile.operator: Regional health authority coordination. Multi-country partnerships.
profile.data_model: Standardized case surveillance data. Cross-border aggregate reports. Harmonized disease classifications.
profile.users_scale: Multiple participating countries and regional health networks.
profile.access_model: Government/institutional access. Participating health authority credentials required.

L1_Surveillance.d: Open-source R package for estimating time-varying reproduction number, growth rate and doubling time of infectious disease outbreaks in real-time
st: Published on CRAN for easy installation in R environments

profile.overview: EpiNow2 is an open-source R package for estimating time-varying reproduction numbers (Rt), case forecasting, and nowcasting, developed by the London School of Hygiene & Tropical Medicine's Centre for Mathematical Modelling of Infectious Diseases.
profile.functional_scope: Real-time epidemic analysis: estimates time-varying Rt from case/death data, performs nowcasting to adjust for reporting delays, generates short-term forecasts, and quantifies uncertainty using Bayesian inference.
profile.tech_stack: R package with Stan for Bayesian MCMC inference. Supports parallel computation. CRAN and GitHub distribution. Integration with EpiEstim, epinowcast. CmdStan backend.
profile.operator: London School of Hygiene & Tropical Medicine (LSHTM), Centre for Mathematical Modelling of Infectious Diseases. Sam Abbott, Sebastian Funk and team.
profile.data_model: Input: time series of case/death counts with onset/report dates. Output: Rt estimates with credible intervals, adjusted case counts, short-term forecasts. All with full posterior distributions.
profile.users_scale: Used by 50+ countries during COVID-19 for official Rt estimation. WHO, CDC, ECDC, and national health agencies. 500+ citations.
profile.access_model: Free and open-source (MIT license). Available on CRAN and GitHub (epiforecasts/EpiNow2). Documentation and vignettes provided.

L1_Surveillance.d: Saudi AI-powered national digital health and pandemic management platform (SDAIA)

profile.overview: Tawakkalna is Saudi Arabia's AI-powered national digital health and pandemic management application developed by the Saudi Data and AI Authority (SDAIA). Became critical infrastructure during COVID-19 with 27M+ users.
profile.functional_scope: National digital health platform: COVID-19 vaccination tracking, health status verification, crowd management, real-time pandemic monitoring, digital health passport, proximity tracing, and integration with national surveillance.
profile.tech_stack: Mobile application (iOS/Android). AI/ML analytics. Cloud infrastructure. Blockchain for health credential verification. Integration with Saudi MOH systems and HESN.
profile.operator: Saudi Data and AI Authority (SDAIA), Riyadh, Saudi Arabia. In coordination with Ministry of Health.
profile.data_model: Individual health records: vaccination status, test results, health risk scores. Crowd density data. Anonymized aggregate analytics for surveillance.
profile.users_scale: 27M+ registered users (majority of Saudi population). Used for Hajj/Umrah crowd management. Critical national digital infrastructure.
profile.access_model: Free government app for Saudi residents and visitors. Mandatory for certain activities during pandemic. Data governed by Saudi data protection regulations.

L1_Surveillance.d: Early Warning Alert and Response Network for emergency/crisis surveillance in MENA region

profile.overview: EWARN (Early Warning Alert and Response Network) is WHO's emergency surveillance system deployed in crisis zones across the Eastern Mediterranean Region (EMRO), providing disease surveillance in conflict and disaster settings.
profile.functional_scope: Emergency disease surveillance in crisis settings: weekly morbidity reporting from health facilities, epidemic alert generation, rapid response team deployment, disease trend monitoring in displaced populations and conflict zones.
profile.tech_stack: Simplified paper-based and electronic reporting systems. Mobile data collection. Epi Info data management. GIS mapping of disease clusters.
profile.operator: WHO Eastern Mediterranean Regional Office (EMRO). Deployed in Syria, Yemen, Iraq, Libya, Somalia, Sudan, and other crisis-affected countries.
profile.data_model: Weekly aggregate morbidity data from sentinel sites. Alert/event reports. Case investigation forms. Simplified syndrome-based reporting (14 priority syndromes).
profile.users_scale: Active in 8+ crisis-affected countries. Hundreds of sentinel health facilities report. Covers millions of displaced and conflict-affected populations.
profile.access_model: WHO emergency program. Data shared with national health authorities and humanitarian partners. Weekly bulletins published. Not publicly accessible at facility level.

L1_Surveillance.d: State-level electronic disease surveillance system for case management, investigation, and reporting of notifiable conditions in US public health jurisdictions

profile.overview: EpiTrax is a state-level electronic disease surveillance system for case management, disease investigation, and reporting used by multiple US states for notifiable disease tracking.
profile.functional_scope: Electronic disease surveillance: case reporting, investigation management, contact tracing, outbreak management, laboratory result integration, and reporting to CDC NNDSS. Workflow management for disease investigators.
profile.tech_stack: Web-based application. Ruby on Rails. PostgreSQL database. HL7 electronic lab reporting (ELR) integration. FHIR compatibility. Open-source.
profile.operator: Utah Department of Health (original developer). Used by multiple US states. Community-maintained open-source project.
profile.data_model: Case-level surveillance records with demographics, symptoms, lab results, exposures, travel history. Investigation workflows. Compliant with NNDSS MMG specifications.
profile.users_scale: Deployed in multiple US states serving millions of residents. Processes thousands of disease investigations annually per state.
profile.access_model: Open-source software. Free for state/local health departments. Technical implementation support. Data access restricted to authorized epidemiologists.

L1_Surveillance.d: Saudi national antimicrobial resistance monitoring program aligned with WHO GLASS

profile.overview: Saudi Arabia's national antimicrobial resistance surveillance program monitors AMR patterns across the country, contributing to WHO GLASS and regional AMR intelligence.
profile.functional_scope: National AMR surveillance: monitors resistance patterns in key bacterial pathogens across Saudi healthcare facilities, provides antibiogram data, informs antimicrobial stewardship programs, and reports to WHO GLASS.
profile.tech_stack: Laboratory information systems integration. WHONET software for AMR data management. Electronic reporting to national AMR database.
profile.operator: Saudi Ministry of Health (MOH), National AMR Committee. WHO GLASS participating country. Saudi CDC coordination.
profile.data_model: Antimicrobial susceptibility testing (AST) results, organism identification, specimen types, facility data. WHONET-compatible format. GLASS reporting structure.
profile.users_scale: Covers major Saudi healthcare facilities. Data from thousands of clinical isolates annually. Reports to WHO GLASS.
profile.access_model: Government surveillance program. Data accessible to authorized MOH personnel. Aggregate national reports published. GLASS data publicly available.

L1_Surveillance.d: China Integrated Surveillance of Disease Control and Prevention - nationwide near-real-time infectious disease reporting system covering 1.4B population

profile.overview: The China Information System for Disease Control and Prevention (CISDCP) is China's national electronic disease surveillance platform operated by China CDC, covering all healthcare facilities for real-time notifiable disease reporting.
profile.functional_scope: National electronic disease surveillance: real-time reporting of 40+ notifiable diseases from all healthcare facilities, automated outbreak detection, sentinel surveillance for influenza and other pathogens, and public health emergency reporting.
profile.tech_stack: Web-based reporting system accessible from all hospitals/clinics. Real-time data transmission. GIS mapping. Automated aberration detection algorithms. National data center at China CDC.
profile.operator: Chinese Center for Disease Control and Prevention (China CDC), Beijing, China. Ministry of Health oversight.
profile.data_model: Case-level data for notifiable diseases: demographics, diagnosis, lab results, onset date, geographic location. Real-time transmission within 24 hours. National aggregate statistics.
profile.users_scale: Covers China's 1.4 billion population. All healthcare facilities report mandatorily. Processes millions of disease reports annually. World's largest infectious disease surveillance system.
profile.access_model: Government system. Accessible to authorized health personnel at all administrative levels. Aggregate data published in Chinese CDC weekly reports. Limited international data sharing.

L1_Surveillance.d: WHO's field-deployable tool for outbreak investigation, including case management, contact tracing, and transmission chain visualization

profile.overview: Go.Data is WHO's free, open-source field-deployable outbreak investigation tool for case management, contact tracing, and visualization of transmission chains during disease outbreaks.
profile.functional_scope: Outbreak investigation: case registration and management, contact tracing and follow-up, laboratory result tracking, transmission chain visualization, GIS mapping, and data export. Supports Ebola, COVID-19, Mpox, and other outbreaks.
profile.tech_stack: Node.js backend with LoopBack framework. Angular frontend. MongoDB database. Works offline (mobile/tablet). REST API. Docker deployment. Interoperability with DHIS2, Epi Info.
profile.operator: WHO Health Emergencies Programme (WHE). Open-source with community contributions. Supported by WHO and GOARN.
profile.data_model: Case records (demographics, symptoms, lab results), contact records, exposure events, follow-up data. Transmission trees. GIS coordinates. FHIR-compatible data exchange.
profile.users_scale: Deployed in 100+ countries. Used during COVID-19 by dozens of national health ministries. Thousands of trained users. 50,000+ person data capacity.
profile.access_model: Free and open-source. Downloadable from who.int/godata. Cloud or local deployment. Training materials freely available.

L1_Surveillance.d: FAO's Global Animal Disease Information System for real-time monitoring and early warning of transboundary animal diseases and zoonotic threats

profile.overview: EMPRES-i (Emergency Prevention System for Transboundary Animal and Plant Pests and Diseases) is FAO's global animal disease information system providing real-time alerts and tracking for transboundary animal diseases.
profile.functional_scope: Global animal disease surveillance: real-time event tracking, disease outbreak mapping, transboundary disease alerts, avian influenza monitoring, foot-and-mouth disease tracking, and One Health event analysis. Integrated with GLEWS+.
profile.tech_stack: Web-based GIS platform. Interactive disease maps. Real-time data feeds from WOAH, FAO country offices, and national veterinary services. API for data access.
profile.operator: Food and Agriculture Organization of the United Nations (FAO), Animal Health Service, Rome, Italy.
profile.data_model: Animal disease events with species, location, date, diagnosis, source. GIS-referenced. Time series of disease incidence. Integration with WAHIS/WOAH data.
profile.users_scale: Global coverage. Used by national veterinary services, WHO, WOAH, and animal health researchers worldwide. Free public access.
profile.access_model: Free public access at empres-i.fao.org. Interactive maps and data downloads. No registration required for basic access. API available.

L1_Surveillance.d: Joint US Forces Korea Portal and Integrated Threat Recognition - portable biological detection and biosurveillance network at Korean military installations

profile.overview: JUPITR (Joint US Forces Korea Portal and Integrated Threat Recognition) is a biosurveillance system for US Forces Korea providing integrated biological threat monitoring on the Korean Peninsula.
profile.functional_scope: Integrated biosurveillance for Korean Peninsula: environmental biological monitoring, syndromic surveillance of military/civilian populations, threat alert dissemination, and coordinated response between USFK and Korean health authorities.
profile.tech_stack: Sensor integration, electronic health record surveillance, environmental monitoring, GIS-based threat visualization, integration with Korean biosurveillance systems.
profile.operator: US Forces Korea (USFK) public health command. Integration with KDCA and Joint ROK-US biosurveillance infrastructure.
profile.data_model: Environmental monitoring data, syndromic surveillance signals, biological detection alerts, health threat assessments. Integrated multi-source intelligence.
profile.users_scale: Covers USFK installations and surrounding areas. Serves force health protection for 28,500+ US troops and associated personnel.
profile.access_model: Military system. Restricted to authorized USFK and ROK military personnel. Not publicly accessible.

L1_Surveillance.d: CDC's free epidemiological analysis toolkit providing questionnaire design, data entry, statistical analysis, and mapping for field epidemiology

profile.overview: Epi Info is CDC's free epidemiological analysis software providing questionnaire design, data entry, and statistical analysis for outbreak investigations and public health surveillance. In use since 1985.
profile.functional_scope: Epidemiological toolkit: customizable questionnaire design, data entry and management, statistical analysis (frequencies, 2x2 tables, logistic regression), GIS mapping, line listing, and epidemic curve generation. Offline-capable.
profile.tech_stack: Windows desktop application (C#/.NET). Access/SQL database backend. Map module with GIS. Mobile companion (Epi Info for Android). Open-source on GitHub.
profile.operator: CDC Division of Health Informatics and Surveillance, Atlanta, GA, USA. Maintained by CDC informatics staff.
profile.data_model: Customizable survey/case report forms. Relational database storage. Import/export: CSV, Access, Excel, XML. Statistical output tables.
profile.users_scale: Used by public health professionals in 180+ countries. Millions of downloads. Standard field epidemiology tool. WHO and FETP training programs worldwide.
profile.access_model: Completely free. Download from cdc.gov/epiinfo. No registration. Open-source on GitHub. Multilingual documentation. Training available.

L1_Surveillance.d: DHS nationwide air-sampling network in 30+ US cities for early detection of aerosolized biological attacks

profile.overview: BioWatch is the DHS nationwide environmental bioaerosol monitoring network deployed in 30+ US cities, providing continuous air sampling for early detection of biological terrorism agents.
profile.functional_scope: Nationwide biological terrorism detection: continuous outdoor air sampling, laboratory analysis for select biological warfare agents (anthrax, plague, tularemia, smallpox, etc.), alerting to federal/state/local authorities, and integration with public health response.
profile.tech_stack: Autonomous air sampling units (dry filter technology). Samples analyzed at Laboratory Response Network (LRN) labs using real-time PCR. BioWatch Advisory Committee for decision support.
profile.operator: Department of Homeland Security, Office of Health Security. Operational 24/7 in coordination with EPA, CDC, FBI, and local first responders.
profile.data_model: Air sample collection metadata, PCR analysis results, BioWatch Actionable Result (BAR) notifications, environmental data (weather, location). Classified detection algorithms.
profile.users_scale: Deployed in 30+ major US metropolitan areas. Covers hundreds of millions of urban residents. DHS's primary environmental biodetection program.
profile.access_model: Government classified program. Specific deployment locations not publicly disclosed. Congressional oversight. Alert protocols involve multiple federal agencies.

st: Global coverage spanning multiple continents and regions
wk: Reporting delays between event occurrence and data availability
L1_Surveillance.d: World Organisation for Animal Health's global information system for official reporting of animal diseases, providing real-time alerts and historical data across 182 member countries

profile.overview: WAHIS (World Animal Health Information System) is the World Organisation for Animal Health's (WOAH, formerly OIE) global information system for animal disease reporting, serving as the official source for animal health status worldwide.
profile.functional_scope: Global animal disease surveillance: immediate notification of 117 listed diseases, 6-monthly reports, annual reports on disease status, antimicrobial use data, wildlife disease monitoring. Official source for international trade health status.
profile.tech_stack: Web-based reporting platform. Interactive dashboards and maps. API for data access. Integration with national veterinary information systems. GIS visualization.
profile.operator: World Organisation for Animal Health (WOAH), Paris, France. 183 member countries and territories report mandatorily.
profile.data_model: Disease events with species, location, date, measures taken. Quantitative data on cases, deaths, vaccinations. Official disease freedom declarations. Standardized WOAH codes.
profile.users_scale: 183 member countries/territories report. Used by FAO, WHO, importers/exporters, veterinary services, and researchers. Official basis for international trade decisions.
profile.access_model: Free public access at wahis.woah.org. Interactive maps and data downloads. No registration for basic access. API available.

L1_Surveillance.c: Next-Gen Epidemic Intelligence from Open Sources
L1_Surveillance.d: WHO upgraded Epidemic Intelligence from Open Sources platform with enhanced AI/ML capabilities for automated event detection across news, social media and official reports in 60+ languages

profile.overview: EIOS (Epidemic Intelligence from Open Sources) is WHO's AI/ML-enhanced epidemic intelligence platform for early detection of public health events. It monitors and analyzes information from diverse open sources including news media, social media, and official reports in multiple languages.
profile.functional_scope: Automated web crawling and aggregation from 150,000+ sources, natural language processing in 50+ languages, event-based surveillance signal detection and categorization, risk assessment and alert generation, geo-tagging and mapping, trend analysis and dashboards, collaborative investigation workflows.
profile.tech_stack: AI/ML pipeline: NLP (entity extraction, sentiment analysis, topic modeling), machine learning classifiers for disease signal detection, web crawling infrastructure. Cloud-hosted with scalable architecture. Custom dashboards and API.
profile.operator: World Health Organization (WHO), in collaboration with the Joint Research Centre (JRC) of the European Commission and other partners in the Epidemic Intelligence Community.
profile.data_model: Structured events: disease, date, location, source, affected population, signal type, verification status. Multi-language text corpus with entity annotations.
profile.users_scale: Used by WHO and 100+ national public health agencies. Monitors 150,000+ sources globally. Processes millions of articles daily.
profile.access_model: Access for WHO and partner public health agencies. Not publicly available. Restricted to authorized users in the Epidemic Intelligence Community.

st: Aligned with OpenHIE architecture for health information exchange
L1_Surveillance.d: Open Health Information Mediator providing an interoperability layer for health information exchange between disparate health systems using OpenHIE architecture

profile.overview: OpenHIM (Open Health Information Mediator) is an open-source middleware platform providing an interoperability layer for health information exchange, connecting disparate health systems in low-resource settings.
profile.functional_scope: Health information exchange middleware: message routing, transformation (HL7v2, FHIR, CDA), security/access control, audit logging, and transaction management. Connects laboratories, hospitals, registries, and surveillance systems.
profile.tech_stack: Node.js backend (OpenHIM Core). React admin console. MongoDB. HL7v2, FHIR R4, CSD, ADX standards. Docker deployment. OpenAPI. Open-source on GitHub (jembi/openhim-core-js).
profile.operator: Jembi Health Systems (South Africa). Open-source community. Part of OpenHIE (Open Health Information Exchange) architecture.
profile.data_model: Health message transactions with routing rules, transformations, and audit trails. Mediator architecture for protocol adaptation. Channel-based message filtering.
profile.users_scale: Deployed in 10+ African countries. Core component of Rwanda HIS, South Africa NDoH systems. Used by WHO, PEPFAR, and Global Fund projects.
profile.access_model: Free and open-source (MPL 2.0). Available on GitHub. Community support. Implementation consulting available from Jembi.

L1_Surveillance.c: AI Epidemic Intelligence as a Service
L1_Surveillance.d: Columbia University health tech startup delivering epidemic intelligence using ML, AI, and geospatial analytics for early detection and prediction

profile.overview: Santé is a Columbia University health technology startup delivering AI-powered epidemic intelligence as a service for public health decision-making.
profile.functional_scope: AI epidemic intelligence: automated disease trend analysis, outbreak risk prediction, integrated epidemiological-genomic analytics, and decision support for public health authorities.
profile.tech_stack: AI/ML analytics platform. Cloud-based SaaS. Integration with multiple data sources. Web dashboard for visualization.
profile.operator: Santé Health Tech, affiliated with Columbia University, New York, NY, USA.
profile.data_model: Multi-source epidemiological data integration. AI-generated risk scores and predictions. Dashboard visualizations.
profile.users_scale: Emerging platform serving public health organizations. Academic research foundation from Columbia University.
profile.access_model: Commercial SaaS model. Partnerships with public health agencies. Academic research component.

L1_Surveillance.d: Clinical surveillance platform integrating electronic health record data with public health reporting for real-time monitoring of disease trends in healthcare settings

profile.overview: MedStar Health is a large nonprofit healthcare system in the Washington DC-Baltimore area whose surveillance capabilities integrate clinical data for syndromic and disease surveillance purposes.
profile.functional_scope: Clinical surveillance: electronic health record (EHR)-based syndromic surveillance, emergency department chief complaint monitoring, laboratory reporting integration, and contribution to regional surveillance networks.
profile.tech_stack: Epic/Cerner EHR integration. Automated syndromic surveillance feeds. Integration with BioSense Platform. HL7/FHIR messaging.
profile.operator: MedStar Health system, Washington DC and Maryland, USA. 10 hospitals, 300+ outpatient sites.
profile.data_model: Syndromic surveillance data from emergency departments and clinics. Chief complaints, diagnosis codes, lab results. De-identified data shared with public health.
profile.users_scale: Serves 4M+ patients in DC-Baltimore region. Contributes to DC Department of Health and Maryland DHMH surveillance.
profile.access_model: Clinical system. De-identified data shared with public health authorities per regulatory requirements. Not publicly accessible.

wk: Geographically limited deployment covering only specific regional areas
L1_Surveillance.d: Electronic Integrated Disease Surveillance System supporting One Health surveillance of both human and animal diseases in Central Asian and Caucasus countries

profile.overview: EIDSS (Electronic Integrated Disease Surveillance System) is a comprehensive surveillance system supporting disease monitoring, sample tracking, and outbreak investigation across multiple countries, particularly in the former Soviet region.
profile.functional_scope: Integrated disease surveillance: human and veterinary disease reporting, laboratory sample management, outbreak investigation, GIS mapping, and antimicrobial resistance monitoring. Bilingual/multilingual support.
profile.tech_stack: Web-based .NET application. SQL Server database. LIMS integration. GIS module. Mobile data collection. HL7 interoperability. DTRA-funded development.
profile.operator: Developed under DTRA Biological Threat Reduction Program. Deployed by national health authorities in partner countries (Georgia, Azerbaijan, Ukraine, Kazakhstan, etc.).
profile.data_model: Human/veterinary disease cases, laboratory samples, outbreak events, geographic data. Customizable data entry forms per national requirements.
profile.users_scale: Deployed in 8+ former Soviet countries. Used by national public health and veterinary services. Thousands of users across deployed countries.
profile.access_model: Government surveillance system in deploying countries. Not publicly accessible. Technical support through DTRA cooperative agreements.

wk: Dated user interface with limited modern visualization capabilities
L1_Surveillance.d: Statistical software for spatial, temporal, and space-time cluster detection using scan statistics, widely used in disease surveillance and outbreak investigation

profile.overview: SatScan is a free statistical software for spatial, temporal, and space-time cluster detection developed by Martin Kulldorff (Harvard/Brigham and Women's Hospital). The most widely used spatial cluster detection tool in epidemiology.
profile.functional_scope: Spatial and temporal cluster detection: scan statistics for identifying disease clusters, prospective surveillance for early outbreak detection, purely temporal, purely spatial, and space-time analyses. Supports Poisson, Bernoulli, and other models.
profile.tech_stack: Standalone Windows/Mac application (Java). Command-line interface. Input: CSV case/population files. Output: clusters with p-values, relative risks, and geographic coordinates. Integration with GIS software.
profile.operator: Martin Kulldorff (currently at University of Denver). Previously Harvard Medical School. Free distribution.
profile.data_model: Input: case locations (coordinates/geo-IDs), population denominators, time periods. Output: detected clusters with statistical significance, relative risk, and geographic boundaries.
profile.users_scale: Downloaded by 100,000+ users. Cited in 5,000+ publications. Used by CDC, WHO, state health departments, and epidemiologists worldwide. Standard tool for cluster detection.
profile.access_model: Completely free. Download from satscan.org. No registration. Cross-platform (Windows/Mac). No restrictions.

st: Extensible plugin architecture supporting custom analytical modules
L1_Surveillance.d: Open-source geographic information system adapted for health applications, providing spatial analysis, mapping, and visualization of epidemiological data

profile.overview: QGIS is the leading open-source geographic information system software, widely adapted for spatial epidemiology, disease mapping, and public health surveillance applications.
profile.functional_scope: Spatial epidemiology and disease mapping: point pattern analysis, disease rate mapping, health facility accessibility, vector habitat mapping, outbreak cluster visualization, and environmental health analysis. Plugin ecosystem for health applications.
profile.tech_stack: C++/Python application. Cross-platform (Windows/Mac/Linux). 1,800+ plugins. Python API (PyQGIS). Integration with R, PostGIS, web mapping. Processing framework.
profile.operator: QGIS.org community project. Open Source Geospatial Foundation (OSGeo). Global volunteer developer community.
profile.data_model: Vector (shapefile, GeoJSON) and raster (GeoTIFF) spatial data. Database connections (PostGIS, SpatiaLite). Web services (WMS, WFS). Health-specific: disease incidence layers, facility locations, population surfaces.
profile.users_scale: Millions of users globally. Standard GIS tool in public health programs worldwide. Used by WHO, CDC, MSF, and hundreds of health ministries.
profile.access_model: Free and open-source (GPL-2.0). Download from qgis.org. No registration. Community support. Training materials freely available.

L1_Surveillance.d: WHO's global web-based tool for influenza virological surveillance, collecting data from National Influenza Centres in the Global Influenza Surveillance Network since 1997

profile.overview: FluNet is WHO's global web-based tool for influenza virological surveillance, providing data on influenza virus types, subtypes, and lineages reported by 150+ National Influenza Centres worldwide.
profile.functional_scope: Global influenza virological surveillance: reports influenza type (A/B), subtype (H1N1, H3N2), lineage (Victoria/Yamagata), from sentinel and non-sentinel sources. Interactive charts and maps. Seasonal and zoonotic influenza monitoring.
profile.tech_stack: Web-based platform. Interactive data visualization. API for data download. Integration with FluID (epidemiological data) and GISRS network.
profile.operator: WHO Global Influenza Programme, Geneva, Switzerland. Data from Global Influenza Surveillance and Response System (GISRS) with 150+ NICs.
profile.data_model: Weekly virological data: specimens processed, influenza positive counts by type/subtype, sentinel vs non-sentinel. Country-level reporting. Historical data from 1997.
profile.users_scale: 150+ countries report. Used by WHO for vaccine strain selection. Referenced by national immunization programs, researchers, and media worldwide.
profile.access_model: Free public access at who.int/tools/flunet. Interactive dashboards. Data downloads (CSV). No registration. WHO FluMart for bulk data access.

L1_Surveillance.d: WHO's Epidemic Intelligence from Open Sources platform for automated detection, filtering, and assessment of public health events from digital media worldwide

profile.overview: EIOS (Epidemic Intelligence from Open Sources) is WHO's AI-powered platform for automated detection and assessment of public health events from open-source information, serving as an evolution of earlier event-based surveillance systems.
profile.functional_scope: Automated epidemic intelligence: AI-powered scanning of global media, social media, official reports, and scientific publications for disease signals. Multi-language NLP. Automated event categorization and risk assessment. Collaborative analysis workspace.
profile.tech_stack: AI/NLP for multilingual text mining and event extraction. Cloud-based analytics. Collaborative web platform. Integration with WHO IHR event management.
profile.operator: WHO Health Emergencies Programme. Partnership with Joint Research Centre (JRC) of the European Commission. Global network of users.
profile.data_model: Disease events extracted from open sources with disease, location, date, source URL, and severity indicators. Collaborative signal assessment. Verified event records.
profile.users_scale: Used by WHO, ECDC, national public health agencies in 50+ countries. Processes thousands of signals daily. Global collaborative network.
profile.access_model: Available to WHO and partner public health agencies. Registration through WHO. Not publicly accessible. Training provided to national focal points.

wk: Coverage limited to EU/EEA member states only
L1_Surveillance.d: European Centre for Disease Prevention and Control's central surveillance platform harmonizing disease reporting across EU/EEA member states with standardized case definitions

profile.overview: The European Centre for Disease Prevention and Control (ECDC) serves as the EU's central hub for disease surveillance, coordinating surveillance data from 30 EU/EEA member states through TESSy and providing risk assessments.
profile.functional_scope: EU-wide disease surveillance coordination: TESSy (The European Surveillance System) data collection for 56 diseases, rapid risk assessments, weekly surveillance reports, epidemic intelligence, Eurosurveillance journal, and technical guidance.
profile.tech_stack: TESSy web-based surveillance platform. EpiPulse for epidemic intelligence. Surveillance Atlas of Infectious Diseases (interactive maps). Publications through Eurosurveillance (peer-reviewed).
profile.operator: European Centre for Disease Prevention and Control (ECDC), Stockholm, Sweden. EU agency serving 30 EU/EEA member states.
profile.data_model: Case-based surveillance data for 56 diseases with standardized reporting formats. Aggregate data published via Surveillance Atlas. ECDC threat assessments and technical reports.
profile.users_scale: Serves 30 EU/EEA countries (~450M population). TESSy receives reports from all member states. Surveillance Atlas publicly accessible. Eurosurveillance widely read.
profile.access_model: TESSy for authorized national surveillance contacts. Aggregate data publicly available through Surveillance Atlas. All publications and risk assessments freely accessible at ecdc.europa.eu.

profile.overview: ABIS refers to the Australian Biosecurity Intelligence Network (ABIN), established with Commonwealth NCRIS (National Collaborative Research Infrastructure Strategy) funding following the 2008 identification of a National Biosecurity Framework as a top government priority. ABIN's mission is to strengthen Australia's biosecurity research, surveillance, diagnostic, and response capability by enabling researchers, industry, and government to collaborate, share data, and generate intelligence using leading-edge tools and technologies through a secure online workspace. It connects CSIRO, state/territory biosecurity agencies, and university researchers.
profile.functional_scope: Collaborative biosecurity intelligence: secure data sharing between researchers/industry/government, pathogen detection analytics, environmental monitoring integration, pest and disease surveillance coordination, diagnostic capability networking, and research collaboration infrastructure for Australia's agricultural and environmental biosecurity.
profile.tech_stack: Secure online workspace platform, CSIRO AI-enabled diagnostic tools, machine learning models for pathogen and pest detection (Object Detection Models), integration with Trellis Intelligence Platform for container screening, federated data sharing infrastructure with role-based access control.
profile.operator: Australian Biosecurity Intelligence Network (ABIN), funded by Australian Government NCRIS. Coordinated with CSIRO, Department of Agriculture, Fisheries and Forestry (DAFF), and state biosecurity agencies. Address: PO Box 225, Dickson, ACT 2602.
profile.data_model: Integrated biosecurity intelligence covering agricultural pests, animal diseases, plant pathogens, and environmental threats. Federated data model allowing cross-agency queries while maintaining data sovereignty.
profile.users_scale: Used by Australian government biosecurity agencies, CSIRO researchers, university biosecurity programs, and agricultural industry partners across all states and territories.
profile.access_model: Secure access for registered Australian biosecurity stakeholders. Requires institutional affiliation. Government-funded, no user fees for participating agencies.

L1_Surveillance.d: Integrated biosurveillance analytics platform combining multiple data sources for comprehensive situational awareness of biological threats and disease activity

st: Free, open API with no authentication required for rapid integration

profile.overview: disease.sh is a free, open-source disease data API that served over 100 billion requests, providing programmatic access to global infectious disease statistics including COVID-19, influenza, and other pathogen data.
profile.functional_scope: RESTful API for disease statistics: global/country/state-level case counts, deaths, recoveries, vaccination data, historical time series, and geographic data. Aggregates from WHO, Johns Hopkins, Worldometer, and government sources.
profile.tech_stack: Node.js backend, Redis caching, MongoDB, hosted on cloud infrastructure. Open-source on GitHub. REST API with JSON responses.
profile.operator: Open-source community project.
profile.data_model: JSON API returning country-level and sub-national disease statistics with temporal data.
profile.users_scale: 100 billion+ API requests served. Used by thousands of applications, dashboards, and research projects.
profile.access_model: Free, open-source, no API key required. Rate limiting for fair use.

L1_Surveillance.d: Open-source RESTful API providing real-time and historical infectious disease data aggregated from WHO, Johns Hopkins, and national health authorities
Africa CDC's continental surveillance platform coordinating disease monitoring and response across 55 African Union member states

profile.overview: Africa CDC's Africa Regional Event-based Surveillance (ARES) platform coordinates continental disease surveillance across 55 African Union member states, providing real-time event monitoring and response coordination.
profile.functional_scope: Continental disease surveillance: event-based surveillance across Africa, outbreak monitoring, cross-border disease intelligence, epidemic preparedness coordination, and support for national public health institutes across AU member states.
profile.tech_stack: Web-based surveillance platform. Integration with national surveillance systems. GIS mapping. Mobile reporting. Coordination with WHO AFRO.
profile.operator: Africa Centres for Disease Control and Prevention (Africa CDC), Addis Ababa, Ethiopia. African Union Commission.
profile.data_model: Disease event reports from 55 AU member states. Aggregate surveillance data. Outbreak situation reports. Regional coordination records.
profile.users_scale: Covers 55 AU member states (1.4 billion population). Coordinates with national public health institutes and Regional Collaborating Centres.
profile.access_model: AU member state access. Weekly bulletins publicly available. Not directly publicly accessible for detailed data. Aggregate reports published.

L1_Surveillance.d: Puerto Rico's territorial public health data portal providing COVID-19 testing results, vaccination data, and disease surveillance statistics

profile.overview: BioPortal is Puerto Rico's territorial public health data portal providing disease surveillance data, COVID-19 tracking, dengue monitoring, and population health statistics for the US territory.
profile.functional_scope: Puerto Rico territorial surveillance: COVID-19 case and vaccination tracking, dengue surveillance, Zika monitoring, notifiable disease reporting, laboratory results, and vital statistics. Interactive dashboards and data downloads.
profile.tech_stack: Web-based dashboard platform. API for data access. Integration with US CDC surveillance systems. Interactive maps and charts.
profile.operator: Puerto Rico Department of Health (Departamento de Salud), San Juan, Puerto Rico. Coordination with US CDC.
profile.data_model: Disease case counts, vaccination records, laboratory results, demographic data for Puerto Rico (~3.2M population). Time series and geographic breakdowns.
profile.users_scale: Serves Puerto Rico health departments, clinicians, researchers, and public. Integration with US national surveillance systems.
profile.access_model: Free public access at bioportal.salud.pr.gov. Interactive dashboards. Downloadable datasets. No registration for public data.

st: Long-term longitudinal surveillance spanning decades of data collection
L1_Surveillance.d: International CJD/prion disease surveillance program based at Edinburgh University, monitoring Creutzfeldt-Jakob disease and related transmissible spongiform encephalopathies

profile.overview: International CJD/prion disease surveillance based at the National CJD Research & Surveillance Unit at the University of Edinburgh, monitoring Creutzfeldt-Jakob disease and other prion diseases globally.
profile.functional_scope: Global prion disease surveillance: monitors variant CJD (vCJD), sporadic CJD, familial CJD, and other transmissible spongiform encephalopathies. Case classification, neuropathological diagnosis, epidemiological analysis, and blood safety monitoring.
profile.tech_stack: Case reporting databases. Neuropathological analysis (Western blot, immunohistochemistry). Epidemiological surveillance. International coordination network.
profile.operator: National CJD Research & Surveillance Unit, University of Edinburgh, UK. International CJD surveillance network. WHO collaboration.
profile.data_model: Case-level CJD data: clinical features, EEG, MRI, CSF biomarkers, genetic testing, neuropathology results. International comparative databases.
profile.users_scale: Monitors CJD cases across 30+ countries. Key resource since BSE/vCJD crisis. Low case numbers but high surveillance sensitivity.
profile.access_model: Academic research unit. Surveillance reports published annually. Case data for authorized researchers. International collaboration network.

L1_Surveillance.d: Open-source electronic medical record system designed for low-resource healthcare settings, supporting disease surveillance through patient-level clinical data collection

profile.overview: OpenMRS is an open-source electronic medical record system designed for resource-constrained environments, widely deployed across sub-Saharan Africa and developing countries for patient care and disease surveillance.
profile.functional_scope: Electronic medical records: patient registration, clinical encounters, medication ordering, laboratory tracking, reporting modules, and integration with surveillance systems (DHIS2). Disease-specific modules (HIV, TB, MCH).
profile.tech_stack: Java/Spring framework. MySQL database. REST/FHIR APIs. Angular/React frontend. Mobile applications. Modular architecture with 500+ community modules. Docker deployment.
profile.operator: OpenMRS Inc. (nonprofit). Global open-source community. Key contributors include Partners in Health, Regenstrief Institute, and community developers.
profile.data_model: Patient-centric data model with concepts, encounters, observations, orders. HL7 FHIR compatible. Concept dictionary for standardized clinical coding.
profile.users_scale: Deployed in 40+ countries. 15,000+ health facilities. Manages 20M+ patient records. PEPFAR-supported HIV programs, PIH sites, and national health systems.
profile.access_model: Free and open-source (MPL 2.0). Available at openmrs.org. Community implementation support. Training materials. Cloud-hosted option (OpenMRS 3.x).

L3_Defense.d: Defense Health Agency program providing global biosurveillance through network of military laboratories in 30+ countries since 1997

profile.overview: GEIS (Global Emerging Infections Surveillance) is the U.S. Department of Defense's infectious disease surveillance network, established in 1997 under the Armed Forces Health Surveillance Division (AFHSD). It operates through a network of overseas military laboratories and partnerships to detect emerging infectious disease threats to military personnel and global health.
profile.functional_scope: Global pathogen surveillance including respiratory diseases, enteric infections, febrile illness, vector-borne diseases, antimicrobial resistance monitoring, and novel pathogen detection. Next-generation sequencing for pathogen characterization. Laboratory capacity building in partner nations. Real-time disease reporting and early warning for force health protection.
profile.tech_stack: Laboratory: WGS (Illumina, Oxford Nanopore), RT-PCR, multiplex assays. Informatics: ESSENCE-based surveillance analytics, custom reporting dashboards, DoD Health-related surveillance data repositories. Integrated with Armed Forces Health Surveillance Branch (AFHSB) data systems.
profile.operator: Armed Forces Health Surveillance Division (AFHSD), Defense Health Agency, U.S. Department of Defense. Supported by HJFMRI (Henry M. Jackson Foundation for the Advancement of Military Medicine).
profile.data_model: Infectious disease event reports, laboratory diagnostic data, pathogen genomic sequences, antimicrobial susceptibility data. Integrated with DoD electronic health records and global disease databases.
profile.users_scale: Operates through 10+ DoD overseas research laboratories on 5 continents. Supports force health protection for 2.8+ million active duty and reserve military personnel.
profile.access_model: DoD internal system. Data shared with partner nations and international public health agencies (WHO, CDC). Published findings in peer-reviewed journals.

L3_Defense.d: US Army Medical Research Institute of Infectious Diseases - premier military biodefense laboratory at Fort Detrick with BSL-4 capabilities

profile.overview: USAMRIID (United States Army Medical Research Institute of Infectious Diseases) is the DoD's lead laboratory for medical biological defense research, located at Fort Detrick, Maryland. Its biosurveillance capabilities support detection, identification, and characterization of biological threat agents.
profile.functional_scope: Research on medical countermeasures (vaccines, therapeutics, diagnostics) against biological threat agents. BSL-2 through BSL-4 laboratory capabilities for working with the most dangerous pathogens. Diagnostic reference laboratory for biological threat agents. Training and consultation for biodefense response.
profile.tech_stack: BSL-4 maximum containment laboratories, whole genome sequencing (Illumina, PacBio, Nanopore), mass spectrometry (MALDI-TOF), PCR-based diagnostics, cell culture and animal model facilities. Integrated with DoD medical surveillance systems.
profile.operator: U.S. Army Medical Research and Development Command (USAMRDC), Fort Detrick, Maryland, USA.
profile.data_model: Pathogen characterization data, diagnostic assay results, genomic sequence data, therapeutic efficacy data. Classified and unclassified data handling.
profile.users_scale: Serves the entire U.S. military and supports national biodefense. Reference laboratory for CDC's Laboratory Response Network (LRN). International partnerships.
profile.access_model: DoD facility with controlled access. Published research in open literature. Diagnostic services available to DoD and federal agencies.

L3_Defense.c: US DoD CBRN Defense Acquisition Executive
L3_Defense.d: Joint Program Executive Office for CBRN Defense - manages $1.6B+ Chemical and Biological Defense Program delivering CBRN defense capabilities to all US services

profile.overview: The Joint Program Executive Office for Chemical, Biological, Radiological, and Nuclear Defense (JPEO-CBRND) is the US Department of Defense's principal acquisition office for CBRN defense equipment and capabilities, managing a $1.6B+ annual portfolio.
profile.functional_scope: Manages development, procurement, and fielding of CBRN defense capabilities: biological detection systems (JBPDS, IBAC), chemical detectors, decontamination systems, medical countermeasures (vaccines, therapeutics, diagnostics), protective equipment, and early warning systems.
profile.tech_stack: Portfolio of defense technologies spanning point detection (bio-aerosol), standoff detection, medical diagnostics, next-gen sequencing (for bioforensics), modeling/simulation tools, and integrated C2 systems.
profile.operator: US Department of Defense, reporting to the Under Secretary of Defense for Acquisition and Sustainment. Located at Aberdeen Proving Ground, MD. Joint service program supporting Army, Navy, Air Force, Marines.
profile.data_model: Acquisition program data, test & evaluation results, threat characterization databases, medical countermeasure stockpile data. CBRN threat libraries and defense capability assessments.
profile.users_scale: Equips all US military branches plus allied forces through Foreign Military Sales. Programs protect millions of service members. Coordinates with BARDA, HHS, DHS for dual-use technologies.
profile.access_model: Government/military acquisition program. Equipment procured through defense contracting. Technical data controlled (ITAR/EAR). Public program information at jpeocbrnd.osd.mil. Congressional oversight reporting.

L3_Defense.d: DARPA program to develop platform that can halt any viral pandemic within 60 days using nucleic acid-encoded medical countermeasures

profile.overview: DARPA P3 (Pandemic Prevention Platform) is a Department of Defense research program aimed at developing an integrated platform to halt viral pandemic outbreaks within 60 days of pathogen identification. Launched in 2017, it focuses on rapid discovery, characterization, production, testing, and delivery of nucleic acid-encoded medical countermeasures.
profile.functional_scope: Rapid antibody discovery from convalescent blood samples (B-cell sorting, single-cell sequencing), antibody characterization and optimization using computational biology, rapid manufacturing of DNA/RNA-encoded antibodies, pre-clinical and clinical testing acceleration, and field-deployable delivery systems. The program demonstrated the ability to discover, manufacture, and begin testing antibody treatments in under 60 days.
profile.tech_stack: Single-cell B-cell sequencing (10x Genomics), computational antibody engineering (Rosetta, machine learning), DNA/mRNA expression platforms, mammalian cell manufacturing (CHO cells), and advanced clinical trial infrastructure. Multiple contractor teams: AbCellera, Duke DHVI, Vanderbilt VUMC, MedImmune/AstraZeneca.
profile.operator: Defense Advanced Research Projects Agency (DARPA), U.S. Department of Defense. Biological Technologies Office.
profile.data_model: Antibody sequence libraries, structural data (cryo-EM, X-ray), binding affinity measurements, neutralization assay results, pre-clinical and clinical trial data. Classified and unclassified data tracks.
profile.users_scale: Multiple contractor teams at Duke, Vanderbilt, AbCellera, MedImmune. Results directly applied to COVID-19 response (AbCellera's bamlanivimab was first FDA-authorized monoclonal antibody).
profile.access_model: Government-funded research program. Results published in peer-reviewed journals. Some technologies transitioned to commercial partners (AbCellera -> Eli Lilly).

L3_Defense.d: European Commission Health Emergency Preparedness and Response Authority - EU crisis response for cross-border biological threats and MCM procurement

profile.overview: The Health Emergency Preparedness and Response Authority (HERA) is the European Commission body established in 2021 to prevent, detect, and rapidly respond to health emergencies. The EU's equivalent to BARDA.
profile.functional_scope: EU-wide health emergency preparedness: threat intelligence and assessment, medical countermeasure development and procurement (vaccines, therapeutics, diagnostics, PPE), stockpiling, manufacturing surge capacity, R&D coordination, and international cooperation.
profile.tech_stack: Regulatory and procurement frameworks. Joint procurement agreements. Emergency framework contracts. Health threat intelligence platform. Integration with ECDC surveillance data.
profile.operator: European Commission DG SANTE, Brussels, Belgium. Works with ECDC, EMA, member states, and industry. Funded by EU budget (multi-billion EUR).
profile.data_model: Health threat assessments, MCM stockpile inventories, procurement contracts, R&D pipeline data. Standardized threat reporting formats aligned with ECDC protocols.
profile.users_scale: Serves all 27 EU member states plus associated countries (~450M population). Coordinates with WHO, US BARDA, international agencies. Manages EU Strategic Reserve.
profile.access_model: Intergovernmental EU body. Public information at EC health portal. Procurement via official EU channels. Intelligence products for EU member state health ministries.

L3_Defense.d: PREventing EMerging Pathogenic Threats - aims to predict and prevent zoonotic spillover events before they reach human populations

profile.overview: DARPA PREventing EMerging Pathogenic Threats (PREEMPT) program aims to prevent viral spillover from animal reservoirs to human populations by developing technologies that can detect and suppress viruses in animal hosts before they make the jump.
profile.functional_scope: Developing technologies to: predict zoonotic spillover events, identify circulating viruses in animal reservoirs, create broad-spectrum antivirals for animal populations, and deploy prophylactic interventions in wildlife to prevent human infection.
profile.tech_stack: Metagenomics for virus discovery, computational spillover risk models, broad-spectrum antiviral development, self-spreading vaccine concepts, environmental DNA sampling.
profile.operator: DARPA Biological Technologies Office (BTO), Arlington, VA, USA. Multiple performer teams from universities and biotech companies.
profile.data_model: Viral surveillance data from animal reservoirs, spillover risk models, antiviral efficacy data, environmental sampling results. Integration with USAID PREDICT virus databases.
profile.users_scale: Research program with multiple performer teams. Results feed into DoD biodefense planning and broader pandemic preparedness. Published in peer-reviewed journals.
profile.access_model: DARPA research program with controlled access. Research results published in open literature. Technology transitions to DoD and civilian agencies upon completion.

L3_Defense.d: Biological Threat Reduction Program - strengthens partner nation biosurveillance and biosecurity across 30+ countries to prevent BW proliferation

profile.overview: The Biological Threat Reduction Program (BTRP) is a key component of the Defense Threat Reduction Agency's (DTRA) Cooperative Threat Reduction program, working with partner nations to counter biological threats through biosafety, biosecurity, and disease surveillance capacity building.
profile.functional_scope: Strengthens partner nation biosurveillance capabilities: upgrades laboratories to BSL-2/3 standards, trains personnel, establishes electronic disease surveillance, provides diagnostic equipment, and builds sustainable public health infrastructure in former Soviet states and other regions.
profile.tech_stack: Laboratory equipment (biosafety cabinets, PCR, sequencing), electronic surveillance systems, training curricula, biosafety/biosecurity assessment frameworks. Integration with national public health infrastructures.
profile.operator: Defense Threat Reduction Agency (DTRA), US Department of Defense. Cooperative agreements with 30+ partner nations. Nunn-Lugar legislation authority.
profile.data_model: Cooperative biological engagement program data: lab assessments, capacity metrics, disease surveillance reports, training records. Integration with WHO IHR core capacity indicators.
profile.users_scale: Active in 30+ countries across Central Asia, Southeast Asia, Africa, and the Middle East. Has upgraded 50+ laboratories. Trained thousands of public health professionals.
profile.access_model: Government-to-government cooperative program. Not publicly accessible. Reports to Congress annually. Some program data shared through unclassified channels.

L3_Defense.d: USAID-funded program that discovered 1,200+ novel viruses across 35 countries by sampling 160,000+ animals - foundational to pandemic preparedness

profile.overview: USAID PREDICT was a pioneering global zoonotic virus discovery program (2009-2020) that identified 1,200+ novel viruses across 35 countries. Led by UC Davis One Health Institute with EcoHealth Alliance, Metabiota, WCS, and Smithsonian.
profile.functional_scope: Zoonotic virus surveillance in wildlife and high-risk human populations. Collected 170,000+ biological samples from bats, primates, and other wildlife. Virus discovery using metagenomic sequencing. Risk characterization and spillover modeling.
profile.tech_stack: PCR-based and metagenomic virus detection. Field sampling protocols for wildlife. Bioinformatics for novel virus characterization. Country-level laboratory capacity building. Training curricula.
profile.operator: USAID-funded ($200M over 10 years). Led by UC Davis One Health Institute. Partners: EcoHealth Alliance, Metabiota (now Ginkgo), Wildlife Conservation Society, Smithsonian Institution.
profile.data_model: Viral sequence data, sample metadata (species, location, date), host ecology data. Published in NCBI GenBank. PREDICT Virus Host Database. Country reports.
profile.users_scale: Operated in 35 countries. Trained 6,500+ people. Strengthened 60+ labs. Discovered 1,200+ novel viruses including new coronaviruses. Data available post-program.
profile.access_model: Program ended 2020. Data and publications freely available. Viral sequences in GenBank. Reports on USAID website. Legacy data continues to inform pandemic preparedness.

L3_Defense.d: UK Defence Science and Technology Laboratory at Porton Down - premier UK biodefense research facility providing CBW defense capabilities to British military

profile.overview: The Defence Science and Technology Laboratory (Dstl) at Porton Down is the UK Ministry of Defence's primary science and technology organization for chemical and biological defense research. Operational since 1916.
profile.functional_scope: CBRN defense R&D: biological agent detection and identification, medical countermeasure development, decontamination technology, protective equipment, threat assessment, forensic analysis. Supports military operations and national security.
profile.tech_stack: BSL-4 containment facilities. Advanced analytical chemistry, genomic sequencing, proteomics, immunology. Environmental sampling and detection systems. Classified R&D programs.
profile.operator: UK Ministry of Defence, Porton Down, Salisbury, Wiltshire, UK. Executive agency of MoD. ~3,500 staff including world-leading CBRN scientists.
profile.data_model: Classified research data on biological agents, countermeasures, and threat characterization. Some publications in open literature. Intelligence products for UK government.
profile.users_scale: Serves UK Armed Forces, Home Office, intelligence agencies, and NATO allies. Supported Novichok investigations (Salisbury 2018). Key partner in Five Eyes CBRN network.
profile.access_model: Government classified facility. Research outputs mostly restricted. Select publications in peer-reviewed journals. External collaboration through contracted research programs.

L3_Defense.d: Joint CBRN Defence Centre of Excellence in Brno, Czech Republic - NATO accredited hub for CBRN defence doctrine, training, and capability development

profile.overview: The NATO Joint CBRN Defence Centre of Excellence is a multinational military organization in Brno, Czech Republic, accredited by NATO to provide education, training, analysis, and lessons learned in the CBRN defense domain.
