profile.overview: DARPA SIGMA+ extends the original SIGMA program to create a networked system of advanced sensors for real-time detection of chemical, biological, radiological, nuclear, and explosive (CBRNE) threats across urban environments.
profile.functional_scope: Urban-scale CBRNE threat detection: distributed sensor networks for city-wide monitoring, real-time data fusion from multiple sensor types (chemical, bio, radiation, explosive), anomaly detection AI, and threat source localization.
profile.tech_stack: Networked sensor hardware (radiation detectors, chemical sensors, bio-aerosol monitors). Edge computing. Cloud analytics. Machine learning for threat detection. GIS integration.
profile.operator: DARPA Defense Sciences Office (DSO), Arlington, VA. Multiple university and industry performers. Transition to DHS and DoD end-users.
profile.data_model: Real-time sensor data streams with geolocation. Threat classification outputs. Fusion products combining multiple sensor modalities. Alert/alarm events.
profile.users_scale: Demonstrated in multiple US cities. Technology transitioning to DHS, DoD protective forces, and law enforcement. Urban sensor networks covering metropolitan areas.
profile.access_model: DARPA research program. Classified and unclassified components. Technology transition to government end-users. Some publications in open literature.

all.d: Canadian SNP-based phylogenomic pipeline for outbreak investigation

profile.overview: SNVPhyl (Single Nucleotide Variant PHYLogenomics) is a Canadian pipeline for SNP-based phylogenomic analysis developed by the National Microbiology Laboratory (NML) of the Public Health Agency of Canada, integrated with IRIDA and Galaxy.
profile.functional_scope: Automated SNP-based phylogenetic analysis for outbreak investigation: reference mapping, SNP calling, core SNP alignment, maximum likelihood phylogeny, and SNP distance matrix. Designed for public health lab use with minimal bioinformatics expertise.
profile.tech_stack: Galaxy workflow. SMALT/BWA mapping, FreeBayes variant calling, PhyML for phylogenetics. Docker containers. Integrated with IRIDA platform. Open-source on GitHub (phac-nml/snvphyl-galaxy).
profile.operator: Public Health Agency of Canada, National Microbiology Laboratory (NML), Winnipeg, Manitoba, Canada.
profile.data_model: Input: paired-end FASTQ reads + reference genome. Output: SNP alignment, phylogenetic tree (Newick), SNP distance matrix, mapping quality metrics. Galaxy history for reproducibility.
profile.users_scale: Standard pipeline for Canadian public health genomic surveillance. Used by PulseNet Canada and international labs. Integrated into IRIDA platform deployments.
profile.access_model: Free and open-source on GitHub. Available as Galaxy workflow. Docker deployment. No registration required. Documentation and tutorials provided.

all.d: DEVCOM CBC/DTRA far-forward portable nanopore sequencing system for pathogen-agnostic identification in field operations

profile.overview: F-FAST (Far-Forward Accelerated Sequencing Tool) and FFBS (Far-Forward Biological Sequencing) are US Army/DTRA programs providing portable nanopore-based DNA sequencing capability for military biological threat identification in field conditions.
profile.functional_scope: Far-forward portable sequencing for biological threat identification: field-deployable nanopore sequencing, rapid bioinformatics for pathogen identification, AMR detection, bioforensics, and sample characterization in austere environments.
profile.tech_stack: Oxford Nanopore MinION/Flongle sequencing. Ruggedized field laptops with offline bioinformatics (minimap2, centrifuge, WIMP). Portable sample preparation kits. Satellite data link for reachback.
profile.operator: US Army DEVCOM Chemical Biological Center (CBC), Aberdeen Proving Ground, MD. DTRA funding. Integration with JPEO-CBRND programs.
profile.data_model: FAST5/FASTQ nanopore reads. Pathogen identification reports. AMR gene detection results. Bioforensic sample characterization. Transmitted via secure military communications.
profile.users_scale: Deployed with US military CBRN units and special operations. Demonstrated in field exercises and real-world operations. Limited to military end-users.
profile.access_model: Military program. Equipment and protocols restricted to authorized DoD personnel. Some capability descriptions in open literature and defense conferences.

all.d: ROK-US syndromic surveillance system for joint biosurveillance on Korean peninsula including Able Response exercises and KDCA integration

profile.overview: The ROK-US Joint Biosurveillance Portal is a bilateral syndromic surveillance system operated by South Korean KDCA and US Forces Korea for joint monitoring of infectious disease threats on the Korean Peninsula.
profile.functional_scope: Joint syndromic surveillance between ROK and USFK: real-time disease monitoring in military and civilian populations, data sharing for outbreak detection, coordinated response planning, and bi-national health threat assessment.
profile.tech_stack: Web-based surveillance portal. Electronic health record integration from military and civilian health systems. Syndromic surveillance algorithms. Bilingual interface (Korean/English).
profile.operator: Korea Disease Control and Prevention Agency (KDCA) and US Forces Korea (USFK) public health command. Bilateral defense agreement framework.
profile.data_model: Syndromic surveillance data from military treatment facilities and Korean hospitals. ICD-coded encounters, chief complaint data, laboratory results. Aggregate trend analysis.
profile.users_scale: Covers USFK personnel (28,500+ troops) and surrounding Korean population. Both military and civilian health data streams. Used for peninsula-wide threat monitoring.
profile.access_model: Bilateral government/military access only. Not publicly available. Operates under ROK-US alliance health cooperation agreements.

all.d: WHO/Wellcome Trust Pandemic Preparedness Analytical Capacity and Funding Tracker - monitors 20,000+ research grants and global pandemic preparedness investments

profile.overview: Pandemic PACT (Pandemic Preparedness Analytical Capacity and Funding Tracker) is a WHO/Wellcome Trust initiative mapping global research funding for pandemic preparedness. Tracks billions in pandemic-related research investments.
profile.functional_scope: Tracks and analyzes global research funding for emerging infectious diseases and pandemic preparedness. Maps funders, recipients, diseases, research categories, and geographic distribution. Enables gap analysis and coordination of pandemic R&D investments.
profile.tech_stack: Web-based analytics dashboard. Database of research grants and contracts. Interactive visualizations (Tableau/D3). Data pipeline for funding database updates.
profile.operator: WHO R&D Blueprint team and Wellcome Trust. Data curation by UKCDR (UK Collaborative on Development Research). Academic partners.
profile.data_model: Research grant records with funder, recipient, amount, disease focus, research category, start/end dates, geographic focus. Standardized disease and research taxonomies.
profile.users_scale: Tracks $10B+ in pandemic research funding. Used by WHO, funders (Gates, Wellcome, NIH), policymakers, and researchers. Open access for global coordination.
profile.access_model: Free public access at pandemicpact.org. Interactive dashboard. Downloadable datasets. Open data policy. API available for programmatic access.

st: Dual-mode access via command-line and web interface
all.d: Standard computational tool for SARS-CoV-2 lineage assignment using the Pango nomenclature system, providing rapid classification of consensus sequences

profile.overview: Pangolin (Phylogenetic Assignment of Named Global Outbreak Lineages) is a software tool and nomenclature system for classifying SARS-CoV-2 genome sequences into epidemiological lineages. Developed by the Rambaut Lab at the University of Edinburgh, the Pango nomenclature is the global standard used by WHO and public health agencies.
profile.functional_scope: Assignment of SARS-CoV-2 sequences to Pango lineages using trained machine learning models (pangoLEARN) or UShER phylogenetic placement. Related tools: Scorpio (constellation-based variant calling), Civet (cluster investigation), Polecat (automated cluster flagging). Lineage designation proposals via GitHub.
profile.tech_stack: Python command-line tool, pangoLEARN (decision tree model trained on curated lineage assignments), UShER (C++ ultrafast phylogenetic placement). Installable via conda/pip. Web version available at pangolin.cog-uk.io. Open-source on GitHub.
profile.operator: Rambaut Lab, University of Edinburgh, with contributions from University of Oxford, University of Sydney, and COG-UK. Funded by Wellcome Trust, MRC, and UKRI.
profile.data_model: FASTA input sequences mapped to Pango lineage designations (e.g., B.1.1.7, BA.2.86). Lineage definitions stored as constellation files with defining mutations.
profile.users_scale: Standard tool used globally - millions of sequences classified. Used by WHO, CDC, ECDC, PHE, and virtually every public health agency worldwide.
profile.access_model: Free, open-source (GPL v3). Command-line tool, web interface, and API. Lineage definitions updated regularly via GitHub.

all.d: Program creating biological capabilities to protect against accidental or intentional misuse of genome editing technologies

profile.overview: DARPA Safe Genes is a research program developing biosecurity tools to protect against misuse of gene editing technologies (CRISPR, gene drives) while preserving their benefits for defense and public health applications.
profile.functional_scope: Biosecurity for gene editing: develops genetic countermeasures to gene drives, anti-CRISPR molecules, safe gene editing tools with built-in controls, and surveillance methods for detecting engineered organisms in the environment.
profile.tech_stack: CRISPR/Cas systems, gene drive constructs, anti-CRISPR proteins, computational genomics for detection of engineered sequences, environmental metagenomics for monitoring.
profile.operator: DARPA Biological Technologies Office (BTO), Arlington, VA. Performer teams from MIT, Harvard, UC Berkeley, NC State, and others.
profile.data_model: Gene drive models, anti-CRISPR molecule libraries, environmental monitoring data, computational detection algorithms for engineered DNA sequences.
profile.users_scale: Research program with multiple academic/industry teams. Results inform DoD biosecurity policy and gene editing governance. Publications in Nature, Science.
profile.access_model: DARPA research program. Published results in open peer-reviewed literature. Some tools and databases shared publicly. IP managed through government contracts.

all.d: SecureBio nonprofit developing pathogen-agnostic metagenomic early warning and LLM-based AI-bio risk assessment tools

profile.overview: SecureBio is a nonprofit developing pathogen-agnostic metagenomic biosecurity technologies and AI biosafety tools. Works closely with the Nucleic Acid Observatory (NAO) at MIT/Broad Institute for early catastrophic biological threat detection.
profile.functional_scope: Metagenomic biosecurity: pathogen-agnostic sequencing of wastewater/environmental samples for novel pathogen detection, AI-based biosafety screening for synthetic biology, early warning of engineered biological agents, and pandemic preparedness analytics.
profile.tech_stack: Metagenomic sequencing (Illumina, Nanopore). Computational pipelines for novel pathogen detection. AI/ML models for DNA synthesis screening. Cloud analytics platform.
profile.operator: SecureBio (501c3 nonprofit), Cambridge, MA. Co-founded by Kevin Esvelt (MIT). Partnerships with MIT, Broad Institute, public health agencies.
profile.data_model: Metagenomic sequence data from environmental samples. Novel sequence detection alerts. DNA synthesis screening results. Integrated with NAO wastewater monitoring data.
profile.users_scale: Growing influence in biosecurity policy. Advises US/UK governments. NAO pilot programs in Boston-area wastewater. DNA screening tools used by synthesis providers.
profile.access_model: Nonprofit with open publications. Some tools and research publicly available. Policy recommendations published openly. Screening tools available to DNA synthesis industry.

all.d: DHS National Biodefense Analysis and Countermeasures Center - BSL-3/4 facility for bio-threat characterization and bioforensic analysis

profile.overview: The National Biodefense Analysis and Countermeasures Center (NBACC) is the US Department of Homeland Security's premier biodefense research facility at Fort Detrick, Maryland, providing biological threat characterization and bioforensic analysis.
profile.functional_scope: Biological threat characterization: studies persistence, virulence, infectivity, and environmental fate of biological agents. National bioforensic analysis: provides definitive analysis for bioterrorism investigations. Supports national biodefense strategy.
profile.tech_stack: BSL-2, BSL-3, and BSL-4 containment laboratories. Advanced genomic sequencing, proteomics, aerobiology chambers. Bioforensic analytical methods. Environmental fate modeling.
profile.operator: DHS Science and Technology Directorate, operated by Battelle National Biodefense Institute (BNBI), Fort Detrick, Frederick, MD, USA.
profile.data_model: Classified threat characterization data. Bioforensic evidence chain-of-custody records. Agent properties databases. Select publications in peer-reviewed journals.
profile.users_scale: Serves DHS, FBI, DoD, intelligence community, and interagency biodefense partners. Provides definitive bioforensic analysis for national-level investigations.
profile.access_model: Government classified facility. Access restricted to cleared personnel. Some research published in open literature. Annual reports to Congress.

all.d: German Bundeswehr CBRN defence capabilities including Institute of Pharmacology and CBRN reconnaissance for biological threat detection

profile.overview: The German Bundeswehr CBRN Defence Command provides the German Armed Forces with comprehensive chemical, biological, radiological, and nuclear defense capabilities, including the Bundeswehr Institute of Microbiology in Munich.
profile.functional_scope: Military CBRN defense: biological agent identification and characterization, field-deployable detection systems, medical intelligence, decontamination operations, protective measures, and rapid response teams. Bundeswehr Institute of Microbiology provides BSL-3 diagnostic reference laboratory.
profile.tech_stack: Mobile CBRN detection laboratories, portable sequencing, rapid PCR diagnostics, decontamination systems, NBC reconnaissance vehicles (Fuchs, DURO). Institute of Microbiology with advanced genomic capabilities.
profile.operator: Bundeswehr CBRN Defence Command (CBRNAbwKdoBw), Bruchsal, Germany. Under Joint Support and Enabling Service. Bundeswehr Institute of Microbiology, Munich.
profile.data_model: Classified military data on CBRN threats, detection results, and operational assessments. Published research from Institute of Microbiology in open literature.
profile.users_scale: Serves German Armed Forces (180,000+ active personnel). Deploys to NATO/EU operations. Key contributor to NATO CBRN battalion. Institute of Microbiology serves as WHO reference lab.
profile.access_model: Military organization. Classified operations. Bundeswehr Institute of Microbiology publishes in peer-reviewed journals. Some training open to NATO allies.

all.d: WHO standardized Joint External Evaluation tool measuring 19 technical areas of IHR compliance including biosafety, biosecurity, and surveillance for 110+ countries

profile.overview: The WHO Joint External Evaluation (JEE) is a voluntary, collaborative assessment tool measuring country capacities across 19 technical areas of the International Health Regulations (2005). First edition 2016, third edition published 2022.
profile.functional_scope: Standardized country assessment across 19 IHR technical areas: antimicrobial resistance, zoonotic diseases, food safety, biosafety, immunization, national laboratory system, surveillance, reporting, workforce, emergency preparedness, and more. Scored 1-5 per indicator.
profile.tech_stack: Structured assessment questionnaire. Standardized scoring rubrics (1=No Capacity to 5=Sustainable Capacity). WHO SPAR self-assessment tool. WHO e-SPAR electronic platform.
profile.operator: World Health Organization (WHO), Health Security Interface. Assessment teams include WHO, peer evaluators from other countries, and external experts.
profile.data_model: Country scores (1-5) across 19 technical areas with 49 indicators. Self-assessment and external evaluation components. Recommendations and priority actions. Published country reports.
profile.users_scale: Over 110 countries completed JEE assessments. Used by GHSA, World Bank, donor governments, and WHO for health security investment prioritization.
profile.access_model: Publicly available country reports on WHO website. Assessment methodology freely accessible. Countries voluntarily participate. No cost for assessment.

all.d: Japan National Institute of Infectious Diseases and JSDF CBRN capabilities including US-Japan biosurveillance innovation challenge

profile.overview: Combined entry for Japan's National Institute of Infectious Diseases (NIID) and Japan Self-Defense Forces (JSDF) CBRN defense capabilities. NIID is Japan's primary infectious disease research institute; JSDF operates the Central Nuclear, Biological, Chemical Weapon Defense Unit.
profile.functional_scope: NIID: national reference laboratory, pathogen surveillance, vaccine development, and public health research. JSDF CBRN: military biological detection, decontamination, medical countermeasures, and rapid response for biological threats.
profile.tech_stack: NIID: BSL-4 facility (Musashimurayama), genomic sequencing, antigenic characterization, epidemiological surveillance systems (NESID). JSDF: NBC reconnaissance vehicles, field detection equipment, protective gear.
profile.operator: NIID: Ministry of Health, Labour and Welfare (MHLW), Tokyo, Japan. JSDF CBRN: Ministry of Defense, Central NBC Weapon Defense Unit, Omiya, Saitama.
profile.data_model: NIID: National Epidemiological Surveillance of Infectious Diseases (NESID) case data, pathogen sequences, vaccine evaluation data. JSDF: classified military threat assessments.
profile.users_scale: NIID serves Japan's 125M population. JSDF CBRN supports 230,000+ active military. Both contribute to regional (ASEAN+3) and global (WHO, GHSA) health security.
profile.access_model: NIID: open publications and surveillance data. JSDF CBRN: military restricted. NESID data publicly available. International collaboration through WHO and bilateral agreements.

all.d: DHS center integrating human, animal, plant, and environmental biosurveillance data through Biofeeds system for national biodefense

profile.overview: The National Biosurveillance Integration Center (NBIC), operated by the Department of Homeland Security, was established by the National Biosurveillance Integration System Act of 2009 to integrate biosurveillance information across federal, state, and local agencies.
profile.functional_scope: Integrates biosurveillance data from human health, animal health, plant health, food safety, and environmental domains. Provides situational awareness, early warning of biological events, and integrated analytical products for decision-makers.
profile.tech_stack: Multi-source data fusion platform. Integrates feeds from CDC, USDA, EPA, FDA, DoD, and state health departments. GIS visualization. Analytical workbenches for threat assessment.
profile.operator: Department of Homeland Security, Office of Health Security, Washington, DC, USA.
profile.data_model: Integrated biosurveillance data: human disease reports, animal disease alerts, food contamination data, environmental monitoring, and agricultural surveillance. Cross-domain analytical products.
profile.users_scale: Serves federal interagency community: DHS, CDC, USDA, DoD, EPA, FDA, intelligence agencies. Provides daily situation reports and special analytical products.
profile.access_model: Federal government system. Products shared with authorized federal, state, local, and tribal officials. Not publicly accessible. Congressional oversight reporting.

st: Integrated quality control checks for sequence data validation
all.d: Fast web-based and CLI tool for quality control, clade assignment, and mutation calling of pathogen sequences including SARS-CoV-2, influenza, and mpox

profile.overview: Nextclade is a web-based and command-line tool for viral genome clade assignment, mutation calling, and sequence quality assessment. Part of the Nextstrain project, it provides instant analysis of SARS-CoV-2, influenza, RSV, mpox, and other pathogen sequences directly in the browser using WebAssembly.
profile.functional_scope: Clade/lineage assignment (Nextstrain clades, Pango lineages, WHO labels), mutation calling (nucleotide and amino acid), phylogenetic placement on reference trees, sequence quality control (missing data, mixed sites, frame shifts, stop codons), and batch processing of thousands of sequences.
profile.tech_stack: Rust core compiled to WebAssembly for browser execution (no server upload needed), React.js frontend, Nextstrain Augur for reference tree generation. Also available as CLI tool. Open-source on GitHub under MIT license.
profile.operator: Nextstrain project (Fred Hutchinson Cancer Center & University of Basel).
profile.data_model: FASTA input sequences analyzed against curated reference datasets per pathogen. Output includes clade assignment, mutation list, QC metrics, and phylogenetic placement.
profile.users_scale: Millions of sequences analyzed. Used by public health labs worldwide for routine SARS-CoV-2 and influenza surveillance.
profile.access_model: Free, open-source. Browser-based (all processing local - no data leaves user's machine). CLI for batch processing.

all.d: Metagenomic sentinel surveillance for early warning of catastrophic pandemics

profile.overview: The Nucleic Acid Observatory (NAO) is a research initiative at MIT and the Broad Institute developing metagenomic surveillance of wastewater and environmental samples for pathogen-agnostic early warning of biological threats, including novel pandemics.
profile.functional_scope: Pathogen-agnostic metagenomic surveillance of wastewater. Sequencing all nucleic acids in samples to detect known and unknown pathogens. Statistical methods for anomaly detection. Goal: provide weeks of early warning for catastrophic biological events.
profile.tech_stack: Deep metagenomic sequencing (Illumina). Computational pipelines for novel sequence detection, anomaly identification, and threat assessment. Cloud computing (Terra/Google Cloud). Custom statistical frameworks.
profile.operator: MIT Media Lab (Kevin Esvelt), Broad Institute of MIT and Harvard, Cambridge, MA. Funded by Open Philanthropy, various foundations.
profile.data_model: Metagenomic sequence data (billions of reads per sample). Taxonomic assignments, novel sequence flags, temporal trend analyses. Environmental metadata (wastewater site, date, flow).
profile.users_scale: Pilot programs in Boston-area wastewater facilities. Growing network. Influential in biosecurity policy community. Publications in Nature, Science.
profile.access_model: Academic research program. Data and methods published openly. Software on GitHub. Sequence data deposited in public archives. Policy recommendations published.

all.d: Global platform for sharing antimicrobial resistance surveillance data from pharma partners

profile.overview: Vivli AMR Register is a global platform for sharing antimicrobial resistance surveillance data, operated by Vivli (a nonprofit data sharing platform) in partnership with pharma companies and academic institutions.
profile.functional_scope: Aggregates and shares AMR surveillance data from pharmaceutical company surveillance programs (Pfizer ATLAS, MSD, etc.) and academic networks. Provides interactive dashboards, data exploration tools, and downloadable datasets for AMR research.
profile.tech_stack: Web-based data platform with interactive dashboards. RESTful API. Data anonymization and harmonization pipelines. Cloud-hosted on Vivli infrastructure.
profile.operator: Vivli Inc. (nonprofit), Cambridge, MA, USA. Data contributors include Pfizer, MSD, Shionogi, academic AMR surveillance networks.
profile.data_model: MIC (minimum inhibitory concentration) data, organism identification, geographic origin, specimen type, year. Standardized across multiple contributing surveillance programs.
profile.users_scale: Millions of AMR data records from global surveillance. Used by WHO, researchers, pharma companies, and public health agencies. Open to qualified researchers.
profile.access_model: Free access to aggregate dashboards at amr.vivli.org. Detailed data access through Vivli data request process. Researcher registration required for full datasets.

all.d: Defence Research and Development Canada Suffield Research Centre - Canadian biological threat defense including live-agent training and NATO ally support

profile.overview: Defence Research and Development Canada (DRDC) Suffield Research Centre is Canada's primary military CBRN defense research facility, located in Suffield, Alberta. Part of the Five Eyes defense research partnership.
profile.functional_scope: CBRN defense research: biological agent detection and identification, chemical warfare agent defense, decontamination technologies, protective equipment evaluation, threat assessment, and environmental monitoring. Supports Canadian Armed Forces and Five Eyes allies.
profile.tech_stack: BSL-2/3 laboratories. Field-deployable detection systems. Environmental sampling and analysis platforms. Chemical detection equipment. Suffield Experimental Proving Ground for open-air testing.
profile.operator: Department of National Defence, Canada. Part of DRDC network. Five Eyes Technical Cooperation Program (TTCP) participant.
profile.data_model: Classified defense research data. Published research in open literature through DRDC technical reports. Alliance-shared threat characterization data.
profile.users_scale: Serves Canadian Armed Forces (68,000+ active). Supports NATO CBRN standards. Collaborates with US (DTRA, Edgewood), UK (Dstl), Australia (DSTG) through Five Eyes.
profile.access_model: Government/military facility. Mostly classified work. Some publications via DRDC reports and peer-reviewed journals. Collaboration through defense agreements.

all.d: Harmonized Hajj Health Information System with Health Early Warning System for Hajj/Umrah

profile.overview: Harmonized Hajj Health Information System (HHIS) with Health Early Warning System (HEWS) provides mass gathering disease surveillance during the annual Hajj pilgrimage in Saudi Arabia, monitoring 2-3 million pilgrims from 180+ countries.
profile.functional_scope: Real-time syndromic surveillance during Hajj: monitoring respiratory, gastrointestinal, and vector-borne diseases. Hospital admissions tracking, laboratory confirmation, environmental monitoring, and rapid response coordination across Hajj healthcare facilities.
profile.tech_stack: Electronic surveillance integrated with Saudi Ministry of Health systems (HESN). Mobile reporting, GIS mapping of disease clusters, laboratory information systems. Integration with WHO IHR reporting.
profile.operator: Saudi Ministry of Health (MOH) in coordination with WHO. Operational during Hajj and Umrah seasons. International health authorities collaborate.
profile.data_model: Syndromic surveillance data from Hajj healthcare facilities. Case-level records, laboratory results, environmental monitoring data. Daily situation reports. Post-Hajj analytical reports.
profile.users_scale: Covers 2-3 million annual Hajj pilgrims from 180+ countries. Hundreds of healthcare workers report. Critical for global health security given mass gathering dynamics.
profile.access_model: Government health system. Internal to Saudi MOH and WHO during Hajj operations. Post-season aggregate reports published. Not publicly accessible in real-time.

all.d: Open repository of US and global disease surveillance data spanning over a century

profile.overview: Project Tycho is an open repository of US and global disease surveillance data spanning 130+ years, developed by the University of Pittsburgh Graduate School of Public Health. Named after astronomer Tycho Brahe for systematic data collection.
profile.functional_scope: Digitized historical US disease surveillance data from 1888 to present (all notifiable diseases). Global disease data from WHO and national sources. Time series of disease incidence by location. Enables analysis of vaccination impact and long-term disease trends.
profile.tech_stack: PostgreSQL database with web interface. REST API for programmatic access. R and Python packages for data retrieval. Standardized data formats (CSV, JSON).
profile.operator: University of Pittsburgh Graduate School of Public Health, Pittsburgh, PA, USA. Funded by Gates Foundation, NIH, and NSF.
profile.data_model: Weekly/monthly disease case counts by US state/city from 1888-present. Standardized disease names, geographic codes, and temporal resolution. 56M+ data points. 90+ diseases.
profile.users_scale: Cited in hundreds of publications including Science, Nature, PNAS. Used by epidemiologists, historians, policy researchers. Free access for academic use.
profile.access_model: Free public access for academic and nonprofit use at tycho.pitt.edu. Registration required. Commercial use license available. API and bulk download.

all.d: Regional health surveillance coordination platform enabling multi-country data sharing and synchronized response for cross-border disease monitoring

profile.overview: SAP in the biosurveillance context refers to regional health surveillance coordination platforms enabling standardized disease reporting and cross-border health intelligence sharing.
profile.functional_scope: Regional surveillance coordination: standardized case reporting formats, cross-border disease intelligence sharing, harmonized laboratory data, and coordinated outbreak response between participating health systems.
profile.tech_stack: Web-based reporting platform. HL7/FHIR interoperability. Dashboard visualization. Secure data exchange protocols.
profile.operator: Regional health authority coordination. Multi-country partnerships.
profile.data_model: Standardized case surveillance data. Cross-border aggregate reports. Harmonized disease classifications.
profile.users_scale: Multiple participating countries and regional health networks.
profile.access_model: Government/institutional access. Participating health authority credentials required.

all.d: Smiths Detection BioFlash Biological Identifier - rapid, sensitive identification of biothreat agents and common diseases using CANARY technology

profile.overview: Smiths Detection BioFlash Biological Identifier is a rapid, sensitive bio-aerosol detection system that autonomously collects, identifies, and alerts on biological warfare agents and environmental pathogens within 15-20 minutes.
profile.functional_scope: Autonomous biological agent detection: continuous air sampling, BAWS (biological agent warning sensor) trigger, immunoassay-based identification, simultaneous detection of multiple agents (anthrax, plague, ricin, botulinum, etc.), and automated alerting.
profile.tech_stack: BAWS aerosol trigger, Lateral Flow Immunoassay (LFI) identification, automated fluidics, ruggedized housing. Serial/Ethernet data output. Integration with CBRN C2 systems.
profile.operator: Smiths Detection (subsidiary of Smiths Group plc), Edgewood, MD, USA / Watford, UK. Military and homeland security customers.
profile.data_model: Alarm/alert events with agent identification, concentration estimates, timestamp, and GPS location. STANAG-compatible data formats. Log files for forensic analysis.
profile.users_scale: Deployed by US DoD, DHS, and allied military forces. Installed at critical infrastructure, military bases, and high-value targets.
profile.access_model: Military/government procurement. Commercial sales through Smiths Detection. Export controlled (ITAR/EAR). Training and maintenance contracts included.

all.d: Battelle Resource Effective Bio-identification System Plus - next-generation airborne biological agent identification under $93M DTRA contract (2026)

profile.overview: Battelle Resource Effective Bio-identification System Plus (REBS+) is a next-generation airborne biological agent identification system providing autonomous detection and identification for military and homeland defense.
profile.functional_scope: Automated airborne bio-agent detection: continuous aerosol collection, trigger and identification using immunoassay and PCR-based methods, multi-agent simultaneous detection, reduced false alarm rates, and integration with command networks.
profile.tech_stack: Aerosol collection/concentration, immunoassay-based identification, miniaturized PCR, automated sample processing. Ruggedized for field deployment. Network-enabled for remote monitoring.
profile.operator: Battelle Memorial Institute, Columbus, OH, USA. Developed under JPEO-CBRND contracts.
profile.data_model: Detection event records with agent classification, confidence level, concentration, timestamp, and location. Integration with CBRN command systems.
profile.users_scale: Deployed with US military CBRN defense units. Evaluated by allied forces. Successor to earlier JBPDS systems.
profile.access_model: Military procurement through JPEO-CBRND. Export controlled. Government-to-government sales for allied nations.

all.d: Bruker comprehensive CBRNE detection portfolio covering chemical, biological, radiation, nuclear and explosives threats for military and civil defense

profile.overview: Bruker's comprehensive CBRNE detection portfolio covers chemical, biological, radiological, nuclear, and explosive threat detection for military, first responder, and homeland security applications.
profile.functional_scope: Multi-spectrum CBRNE detection: chemical warfare agent identification (IMS, GC-MS), biological agent detection (rapid PCR, immunoassay), radiation detection, explosives screening. Includes DE-ttte, RAPIDPlus, and other systems.
profile.tech_stack: Ion Mobility Spectrometry (IMS), GC-MS, real-time PCR, immunochromatographic assays, gamma/neutron detectors. Portable to vehicle-mounted form factors.
profile.operator: Bruker Corporation CBRNE Division, Leipzig, Germany / Billerica, MA, USA. Global defense and security customer base.
profile.data_model: Agent identification results with confidence scores, concentration measurements, spectral data. Export to CBRN C2 systems. Forensic data packages.
profile.users_scale: Used by NATO militaries, European first responders, airport security, and industrial safety. Major defense exhibitions presence globally.
profile.access_model: Commercial defense sales. Export controlled (dual-use). Service contracts and training included. Government procurement processes.

all.d: UK's COVID-19 Genomics Consortium that sequenced over 2 million SARS-CoV-2 genomes, establishing the blueprint for national pathogen genomic surveillance

profile.overview: COG-UK (COVID-19 Genomics UK Consortium) was a national consortium for SARS-CoV-2 genome sequencing in the United Kingdom, established in March 2020 and closed on March 31, 2023. It sequenced over 2.5 million SARS-CoV-2 genomes, making the UK the world leader in COVID-19 genomic surveillance.
profile.functional_scope: Large-scale SARS-CoV-2 whole genome sequencing, real-time variant detection and tracking, integration of genomic data with epidemiological information, generation of phylogenetic analyses for outbreak investigation, informing public health policy and vaccine strategy. Developed Pangolin, Civet, and other tools.
profile.tech_stack: Oxford Nanopore and Illumina sequencing. ARTIC protocol for amplicon-based WGS. CLIMB (Cloud Infrastructure for Microbial Bioinformatics) for data processing. Custom bioinformatics pipelines. Data shared via GISAID and ENA.
profile.operator: UK Health Security Agency (UKHSA), Wellcome Sanger Institute, University of Edinburgh, University of Oxford, and 20+ partner institutions. Funded by UKRI/MRC, DHSC, and Wellcome Trust.
profile.data_model: SARS-CoV-2 consensus sequences with lineage assignments, sample metadata (collection date, region, patient demographics), and epidemiological linkage data.
profile.users_scale: Sequenced 2.5+ million SARS-CoV-2 genomes (largest national sequencing program globally). Consortium of 20+ academic and public health institutions.
profile.access_model: Closed program (archived). Sequencing data publicly available via GISAID and ENA. Tools (Pangolin, etc.) remain actively maintained and open-source. Website archived by UK National Archives.

all.d: INTERfering and Co-Evolving Prevention and Therapy - uses viral evolution to create adaptive medical countermeasures

profile.overview: DARPA INTERCEPT (INTERfering and Co-Evolving Prevention and Therapy) uses viral vectors to create therapeutic interfering particles (TIPs) that can outcompete and suppress pathogenic viruses.
profile.functional_scope: Develops defective interfering particles that parasitize pathogenic viruses, using the virus's own replication machinery against it. Aims to create self-amplifying antivirals that evolve alongside target pathogens.
profile.tech_stack: Synthetic biology for defective interfering particle design, viral vector engineering, computational coevolution modeling, in vivo efficacy testing.
profile.operator: DARPA Biological Technologies Office (BTO), Arlington, VA. Academic and biotech performer teams.
profile.data_model: TIP design parameters, viral competition models, in vivo efficacy data, evolutionary trajectory predictions.
profile.users_scale: Research program. Results feed into broad-spectrum antiviral development. Publications in leading journals.
profile.access_model: DARPA research program. Published results in open literature. IP managed through government contracts.

all.d: Open-source R package for estimating time-varying reproduction number, growth rate and doubling time of infectious disease outbreaks in real-time
st: Published on CRAN for easy installation in R environments

profile.overview: EpiNow2 is an open-source R package for estimating time-varying reproduction numbers (Rt), case forecasting, and nowcasting, developed by the London School of Hygiene & Tropical Medicine's Centre for Mathematical Modelling of Infectious Diseases.
profile.functional_scope: Real-time epidemic analysis: estimates time-varying Rt from case/death data, performs nowcasting to adjust for reporting delays, generates short-term forecasts, and quantifies uncertainty using Bayesian inference.
profile.tech_stack: R package with Stan for Bayesian MCMC inference. Supports parallel computation. CRAN and GitHub distribution. Integration with EpiEstim, epinowcast. CmdStan backend.
profile.operator: London School of Hygiene & Tropical Medicine (LSHTM), Centre for Mathematical Modelling of Infectious Diseases. Sam Abbott, Sebastian Funk and team.
profile.data_model: Input: time series of case/death counts with onset/report dates. Output: Rt estimates with credible intervals, adjusted case counts, short-term forecasts. All with full posterior distributions.
profile.users_scale: Used by 50+ countries during COVID-19 for official Rt estimation. WHO, CDC, ECDC, and national health agencies. 500+ citations.
profile.access_model: Free and open-source (MIT license). Available on CRAN and GitHub (epiforecasts/EpiNow2). Documentation and vignettes provided.

all.d: Israel's premier classified biological research institute at Ness Ziona - national biodefense against intentional attacks and emerging diseases

profile.overview: The Israel Institute for Biological Research (IIBR) is Israel's premier classified biological research institute located in Ness Ziona, under the Prime Minister's Office. Conducts defense-oriented research in biology, chemistry, and environmental science.
profile.functional_scope: Classified biodefense R&D: vaccines (developed IIBR COVID-19 vaccine candidate), antidotes, diagnostic tools, protective measures against biological and chemical threats. Environmental monitoring and decontamination technologies.
profile.tech_stack: BSL-4 containment facilities. Advanced molecular biology, immunology, analytical chemistry, computational biology. Clinical development capabilities. Manufacturing pilot plants.
profile.operator: Prime Minister's Office, State of Israel. Located in Ness Ziona. ~350 researchers and scientists.
profile.data_model: Classified research data. Select publications in peer-reviewed journals. Patent filings for non-classified technologies.
profile.users_scale: Serves Israel Defense Forces, Israeli national security agencies, and Ministry of Health. Key contributor to Israeli pandemic response (COVID-19 vaccines, diagnostics).
profile.access_model: Classified government facility. Limited public information. Some publications in open literature. Products (vaccines, diagnostics) available through Israeli government channels.

all.d: Chemring Sensors tactical biological detector and Joint Biological Tactical Detection System for advanced point bio-agent detection with reduced false alarms
st: TACBIO 2 patent agreement with DEVCOM CBC
Lowest false alarm rate in class

profile.overview: Chemring Sensors TACBIO (Tactical Biological detector) and Joint Biological Tactical Detection System (JBTDS) provide advanced biological point detection capability for military forces, identifying biological warfare agents in near-real-time.
profile.functional_scope: Tactical biological agent detection: continuous aerosol sampling, UV fluorescence trigger, immunoassay-based identification of multiple biological warfare agents. JBTDS is the US program of record succeeding JBPDS.
profile.tech_stack: UV particle fluorescence trigger, lateral flow immunoassay identification, automated sample processing. Ruggedized for mounted/dismounted operations. STANAG-compatible C2 integration.
profile.operator: Chemring Sensors (Chemring Group plc), Charlotte, NC, USA. Under US Army / JPEO-CBRND contract.
profile.data_model: Biological detection events with agent classification, concentration estimates, timestamp, and geolocation. Command network integration. Alert/alarm protocols.
profile.users_scale: US Army program of record. Being fielded to combat brigades. Evaluated by NATO allies.
profile.access_model: Military procurement. Export controlled (ITAR). Government-to-government foreign military sales.

st: Browser-based interface requiring no local installation
all.d: Web-based bioinformatics platform for influenza and SARS-CoV-2 whole-genome sequencing analysis, providing automated variant calling and phylogenetic analysis

profile.overview: INSaFLU is a free, web-based bioinformatics platform for influenza and SARS-CoV-2 whole genome sequencing analysis. Developed by the National Institute of Health (INSA) of Portugal, it provides automated pipelines from raw reads to phylogenetic analysis.
profile.functional_scope: Automated reference-based genome assembly, variant calling (SNPs and indels), consensus sequence generation, clade/lineage assignment, minor variant detection (intra-host diversity), phylogenetic tree building, and surveillance reporting. Supports both Illumina and Oxford Nanopore data.
profile.tech_stack: Web platform built on Django (Python), using bioinformatics tools: snippy (variant calling), MAFFT (alignment), FastTree/IQ-TREE (phylogenetics), Abricate (AMR), Nextclade integration. Docker deployment. Open-source on GitHub.
profile.operator: National Institute of Health Doutor Ricardo Jorge (INSA), Lisbon, Portugal.
profile.data_model: Raw reads (FASTQ) and reference genomes as input. Outputs: consensus sequences, variant tables, phylogenetic trees, quality reports. User-managed private workspace.
profile.users_scale: Used by public health laboratories in 30+ countries for influenza and SARS-CoV-2 genomic surveillance.
profile.access_model: Free. Account required for upload and analysis. User data stored privately. No sharing of submitted data by INSaFLU team.

all.d: Teledyne FLIR Instantaneous Bioaerosol Analysis and Collection system detecting all four classes of biological threats (spore, viral, cellular, toxin)

profile.overview: Teledyne FLIR IBAC 2 (Instantaneous Bioaerosol Analysis and Collection 2) is a real-time biological aerosol warning sensor that detects hazardous bio-particles in the air, providing early trigger capability for biological defense.
profile.functional_scope: Continuous bio-aerosol monitoring: UV laser-induced fluorescence for real-time particle detection, automatic sample collection for confirmatory analysis, early warning trigger for downstream identification systems, environmental background discrimination.
profile.tech_stack: UV laser-induced fluorescence (UV-LIF), aerosol particle counting, automatic sample collection cassettes. Ethernet/RS-232 data output. Integration with CBRN C2 networks.
profile.operator: Teledyne FLIR Defense, Wilsonville, OR, USA (formerly FLIR Systems). Military and critical infrastructure customers.
profile.data_model: Real-time particle concentration data, fluorescence characteristics, alarm events, collected sample metadata. Time-stamped event logs.
profile.users_scale: Widely deployed by US DoD, allied militaries, and critical infrastructure protection. Key component of layered biological defense systems.
profile.access_model: Military/government procurement through defense channels. Export controlled. Training and lifecycle support contracts.

all.d: Gryphon Scientific/DTRA (now Deloitte) Disease Outbreak Risk Assessment tool for AI-driven country-to-country disease spread prediction and GOF risk analysis

profile.overview: DORA (Disease Outbreak Risk Assessment) is an AI-powered outbreak risk prediction tool originally developed by Gryphon Scientific under DTRA contract, now part of Deloitte's biotech consulting portfolio.
profile.functional_scope: AI/ML-based prediction of disease outbreak risk: analyzes epidemiological, environmental, socioeconomic, and political factors to forecast outbreak likelihood and severity for specific regions and pathogens.
profile.tech_stack: Machine learning models (random forests, neural networks), geospatial analysis, multi-source data integration, cloud analytics platform.
profile.operator: Originally Gryphon Scientific (now Deloitte) under DTRA contract. US government defense and intelligence community customers.
profile.data_model: Multi-dimensional risk scores incorporating epidemiological data, climate variables, healthcare capacity, political stability, population density, and historical outbreak patterns.
profile.users_scale: Used by DTRA, DoD combatant commands, and intelligence community for biological threat assessment. Informs defense planning and resource allocation.
profile.access_model: Government contract access. Classified/restricted to authorized US government users. Some methodological publications in open literature.

all.d: Nucleic acids On-demand Worldwide - mobile platform for rapid field production of nucleic acid medical countermeasures

profile.overview: DARPA NOW (Nucleic acids On-demand Worldwide) develops mobile platforms for rapid manufacture of nucleic acid-based medical countermeasures in austere environments, enabling field production of vaccines and therapeutics.
profile.functional_scope: Field-deployable mRNA/DNA manufacturing: portable systems to produce nucleic acid-based vaccines, therapeutics, and diagnostics on-demand at point of need. Eliminates cold chain requirements.
profile.tech_stack: Enzymatic DNA/RNA synthesis, in vitro transcription, microfluidics, portable purification systems. Ruggedized field-deployable form factor.
profile.operator: DARPA Biological Technologies Office (BTO), Arlington, VA. Academic and biotech performer teams.
profile.data_model: Manufacturing process parameters, nucleic acid sequence specifications, quality control data, field deployment logs.
profile.users_scale: Research/development program. Technology transition to military medical logistics. Potential for humanitarian and pandemic response deployment.
profile.access_model: DARPA research program. Some technologies approaching transition. Defense acquisition pathway.

all.d: JPEO-CBRND Enhanced Maritime Biological Detection system - upgrade to JBPDS providing automated airborne bio-agent detection and identification for US Navy ships
st: Near real-time bio detection at sea

profile.overview: Enhanced Maritime Biological Detection (EMBD) is a JPEO-CBRND program providing shipboard biological agent detection capability for US Navy vessels, enabling continuous monitoring for biological threats at sea.
profile.functional_scope: Shipboard biological defense: continuous aerosol sampling, automated biological agent detection and identification, integration with ship combat systems, crew protection alerting.
profile.tech_stack: Aerosol sampling/collection adapted for maritime environment, immunoassay identification, integration with ship's Damage Control and NBC systems. Ruggedized for naval operations.
profile.operator: JPEO-CBRND, US Navy. Installed on aircraft carriers, amphibious ships, and other major combatants.
profile.data_model: Biological detection events, agent classification, ship environmental data, alert protocols. Integration with Navy tactical data systems.
profile.users_scale: US Navy fleet-wide deployment program. Carrier strike groups and expeditionary forces.
profile.access_model: Military program. US Navy procurement. Export controlled for allied navies through Foreign Military Sales.

all.d: JPEO-CBRND next-generation man-portable chemical/aerosol agent detector by Smiths Detection for joint force CBRN defense with biological detection extension

profile.overview: AVCAD (Aerosol Vapor Chemical Agent Detector) is JPEO-CBRND's next-generation man-portable chemical and aerosol agent detection system, designed to replace legacy detectors with improved sensitivity and reduced false alarm rates.
profile.functional_scope: Portable CBRN detection: chemical warfare agent vapor detection, aerosol threat detection, toxic industrial chemical identification, reduced false alarm rate, multi-threat simultaneous detection capability.
profile.tech_stack: Advanced ion mobility spectrometry (IMS), surface acoustic wave (SAW) sensors, miniaturized electronics. Battery-powered man-portable form factor. Data link to C2 systems.
profile.operator: JPEO-CBRND, US Army. Developed for dismounted soldier and vehicle-mounted use across all services.
profile.data_model: Chemical/aerosol detection events, agent classification, concentration levels, GPS coordinates, timestamp. Integration with CBRN warning/reporting systems.
profile.users_scale: US military program of record replacing JCAD. All service branches. NATO interoperable.
profile.access_model: Military acquisition through JPEO-CBRND. Export controlled. Allied procurement through FMS.

all.d: ECDC's Genomic Epidemiology Coordination platform for EU/EEA-wide genomic surveillance standardization, harmonizing pathogen sequencing and data sharing across member states

profile.overview: GECO (Genomic Epidemiology Coordination) is the European Centre for Disease Prevention and Control's platform for coordinating genomic surveillance across EU/EEA member states, facilitating cross-border pathogen tracking.
profile.functional_scope: EU-wide genomic surveillance coordination: standardized sequencing protocols, cross-border sequence data sharing, variant monitoring, cluster detection, and integrated epidemiological-genomic analysis for EU/EEA member states.
profile.tech_stack: TESSy (The European Surveillance System) integration. Sequencing data standards. ECDC pathogen-specific platforms. Integration with GISAID and ENA.
profile.operator: European Centre for Disease Prevention and Control (ECDC), Stockholm, Sweden. All 30 EU/EEA member states participate.
profile.data_model: Pathogen genomic sequences with structured metadata (country, date, specimen type). Linked with TESSy epidemiological data. ECDC-standardized reporting formats.
profile.users_scale: Serves 30 EU/EEA countries (~450M population). Coordinates national reference laboratories. Key tool for EU pandemic response.
profile.access_model: EU/EEA member state access through ECDC portal. Aggregate data publicly available through ECDC surveillance reports.

all.d: JPEO-CBRND automated airborne biological agent detection system deployed on ships and installations for continuous monitoring

profile.overview: The Joint Biological Point Detection System (JBPDS) is the US military's fielded automated biological agent detection and identification system, providing 24/7 unattended monitoring for biological warfare agents at fixed and semi-fixed locations.
profile.functional_scope: Automated biological defense: continuous aerosol trigger (UV-LIF), sample collection, immunoassay-based agent identification, automated alerting to commanders. Detects multiple bio-warfare agents simultaneously.
profile.tech_stack: UV-LIF trigger (IBAC), aerosol collector, immunoassay identification (BioFlash-type), automated sample processing. Ethernet/SIPR network integration. Ruggedized shelter.
profile.operator: JPEO-CBRND, US Army. Fielded with CBRN units across all services and combatant commands.
profile.data_model: Biological detection events, agent identification reports, alarm history, environmental background data. CBRN warning/reporting message formats (NBC-1, NBC-3).
profile.users_scale: Fielded across US military installations, deployed locations, and allied nations. Hundreds of systems in service.
profile.access_model: Military procurement. Export controlled (FMS for allies). JPEO-CBRND lifecycle management.

all.d: Rheinmetall mobile NBC field laboratories for military biological agent analysis and CBRN reconnaissance deployed by Bundeswehr and NATO allies

profile.overview: Rheinmetall mobile NBC Field Laboratories provide military forces with deployable biological and chemical analysis capability for CBRN reconnaissance, sample analysis, and threat identification in the field.
profile.functional_scope: Mobile CBRN reconnaissance and analysis: deployable containerized laboratory for biological sample analysis, chemical agent identification, environmental monitoring, and forensic sampling. Rapid deployment capability.
profile.tech_stack: Containerized/vehicle-mounted laboratory. PCR, immunoassay, chemical analysis instruments, biosafety containment. NBC reconnaissance vehicles (Fuchs/Boxer integration).
profile.operator: Rheinmetall Defence, Düsseldorf, Germany. Customers include Bundeswehr, NATO allies, and Middle Eastern/Asian militaries.
profile.data_model: Laboratory analysis results, sample chain-of-custody, CBRN reconnaissance reports. NATO STANAG-compatible reporting.
profile.users_scale: Deployed by German Bundeswehr and exported to 10+ NATO and partner nations. Part of NATO CBRN battalion capabilities.
profile.access_model: Military procurement through Rheinmetall Defence. Export controlled. Training and lifecycle support included.

all.d: Saudi AI-powered national digital health and pandemic management platform (SDAIA)

profile.overview: Tawakkalna is Saudi Arabia's AI-powered national digital health and pandemic management application developed by the Saudi Data and AI Authority (SDAIA). Became critical infrastructure during COVID-19 with 27M+ users.
profile.functional_scope: National digital health platform: COVID-19 vaccination tracking, health status verification, crowd management, real-time pandemic monitoring, digital health passport, proximity tracing, and integration with national surveillance.
profile.tech_stack: Mobile application (iOS/Android). AI/ML analytics. Cloud infrastructure. Blockchain for health credential verification. Integration with Saudi MOH systems and HESN.
profile.operator: Saudi Data and AI Authority (SDAIA), Riyadh, Saudi Arabia. In coordination with Ministry of Health.
profile.data_model: Individual health records: vaccination status, test results, health risk scores. Crowd density data. Anonymized aggregate analytics for surveillance.
profile.users_scale: 27M+ registered users (majority of Saudi population). Used for Hajj/Umrah crowd management. Critical national digital infrastructure.
profile.access_model: Free government app for Saudi residents and visitors. Mandatory for certain activities during pandemic. Data governed by Saudi data protection regulations.

all.d: Asia Pathogen Genomics Initiative for regional genomic surveillance capacity

profile.overview: The Asia Pathogen Genomics Initiative (APGI) is a regional capacity-building program for genomic surveillance of infectious diseases across Asia, supported by international partners.
profile.functional_scope: Regional genomic surveillance capacity building: training public health laboratories in sequencing, bioinformatics, data sharing, and genomic epidemiology. Supports pathogen genomic surveillance networks across Asian countries.
profile.tech_stack: Sequencing technology training (Illumina, Nanopore). Bioinformatics pipeline deployment. Data sharing infrastructure. Quality assurance protocols.
profile.operator: Multi-partner initiative with support from WHO, USCDC, and Asian Development Bank. Regional coordination across Southeast and South Asian countries.
profile.data_model: Pathogen genomic sequences with epidemiological metadata. Harmonized data standards. Regional sequence databases.
profile.users_scale: Participating laboratories across multiple Asian countries. Building capacity for routine genomic surveillance in the region.
profile.access_model: Institutional participation through national public health agencies. Training and resources provided to member countries.

all.d: Epigenetic CHaracterization and Observation - detects prior WMD exposure through epigenetic signatures

profile.overview: DARPA ECHO (Epigenetic CHaracterization and Observation) detects prior exposure to weapons of mass destruction (chemical, biological, radiological) through epigenetic signatures in blood samples, even long after exposure.
profile.functional_scope: WMD exposure detection through epigenetic biomarkers: identifies DNA methylation and histone modification patterns indicating past exposure to biological agents, chemical weapons, or radiation. Forensic and intelligence applications.
profile.tech_stack: Epigenomics (bisulfite sequencing, ChIP-seq), machine learning for biomarker discovery, portable blood sampling, computational epigenetic analysis.
profile.operator: DARPA Biological Technologies Office (BTO), Arlington, VA. Academic and biotech performer teams.
profile.data_model: Epigenetic profiles (methylation patterns), exposure biomarker databases, classification algorithms. Blood sample metadata.
profile.users_scale: Research program. Results feed into DoD medical intelligence and forensic investigation capabilities. WMD treaty verification potential.
profile.access_model: DARPA research program. Some results in open literature. Defense/intelligence community applications.

all.d: Rapid Threat Assessment - speeds production of medical countermeasures for US forces by rapidly characterizing unknown bio-agents

profile.overview: DARPA RTA (Rapid Threat Assessment) speeds production of medical countermeasures by rapidly characterizing novel biological threats' mechanisms of action, reducing the traditional 5-10 year timeline.
profile.functional_scope: Rapid biological threat characterization: determines how novel biological agents cause disease at molecular level, identifies therapeutic targets, and accelerates MCM development timelines from years to months.
profile.tech_stack: High-throughput -omics (transcriptomics, proteomics, metabolomics), organ-on-chip models, computational biology, AI-driven drug target identification.
profile.operator: DARPA Biological Technologies Office (BTO), Arlington, VA. Multiple university and industry performer teams.
profile.data_model: Multi-omics datasets, mechanism-of-action models, therapeutic target databases, MCM development timelines.
profile.users_scale: Research program feeding into BARDA/JPEO-CBRND MCM development. Results published in peer-reviewed journals.
profile.access_model: DARPA research program. Published results in open literature. Technology transition to defense medical acquisition.

all.d: DARPA program developing integrated platform for rapid prediction, synthesis and testing of novel multi-target therapeutics for warfighter physiological needs

profile.overview: DARPA PANACEA develops an integrated platform for rapid prediction, identification, and production of multi-target therapeutics against biological threats, aiming for broad-spectrum treatments deployable before threat identification.
profile.functional_scope: Multi-target therapeutic platform: computational prediction of broad-spectrum drug targets, rapid screening of candidate molecules, accelerated preclinical development, and manufacturing of combination therapeutics against multiple biological agents.
profile.tech_stack: AI/ML for drug target prediction, high-throughput screening, structural biology, automated drug synthesis, preclinical efficacy models.
profile.operator: DARPA Biological Technologies Office (BTO), Arlington, VA. Academic and pharmaceutical performer teams.
profile.data_model: Drug target databases, compound screening results, structure-activity relationships, preclinical efficacy data, manufacturing specifications.
profile.users_scale: Research program. Aims to create deployable broad-spectrum therapeutics for military and pandemic use.
profile.access_model: DARPA research program. Published results in open literature. Technology transition to defense medical systems.

st: Comprehensive training materials and capacity-building resources
all.d: Open-source R package ecosystem (TRACE initiative) providing modular tools for outbreak analytics, disease modeling, and genomic epidemiology in R/Python

profile.overview: EpiVerse TRACE (Tools for Real-time Analytics of Epidemics and Collaborative Epidemiology) is an open-source R package ecosystem for outbreak analytics, developed by a consortium funded by data.org and London School of Hygiene & Tropical Medicine.
profile.functional_scope: Suite of R packages for epidemic analysis: outbreak reconstruction, transmission chain analysis, genomic-epidemiological integration, forecasting, severity estimation, and contact tracing data analysis. Designed for field epidemiologists.
profile.tech_stack: R packages (R/C++). Includes linelist (data cleaning), epiparameter (pathogen parameters), cfr (case fatality), epichains (transmission chains). GitHub-hosted. CI/CD tested.
profile.operator: LSHTM, data.org, Imperial College London, Jagiellonian University, and international consortium. Funded by data.org.
profile.data_model: Standardized line list data, transmission trees, contact tracing records, genomic metadata. Interoperable with WHO GOARN data formats.
profile.users_scale: Growing adoption by WHO, CDC, and field epidemiologists. Active open-source community. Training workshops delivered globally.
profile.access_model: Free and open-source (MIT license). All packages on CRAN and GitHub (epiverse-trace). Comprehensive documentation and tutorials.

all.d: Early Warning Alert and Response Network for emergency/crisis surveillance in MENA region

profile.overview: EWARN (Early Warning Alert and Response Network) is WHO's emergency surveillance system deployed in crisis zones across the Eastern Mediterranean Region (EMRO), providing disease surveillance in conflict and disaster settings.
profile.functional_scope: Emergency disease surveillance in crisis settings: weekly morbidity reporting from health facilities, epidemic alert generation, rapid response team deployment, disease trend monitoring in displaced populations and conflict zones.
profile.tech_stack: Simplified paper-based and electronic reporting systems. Mobile data collection. Epi Info data management. GIS mapping of disease clusters.
profile.operator: WHO Eastern Mediterranean Regional Office (EMRO). Deployed in Syria, Yemen, Iraq, Libya, Somalia, Sudan, and other crisis-affected countries.
profile.data_model: Weekly aggregate morbidity data from sentinel sites. Alert/event reports. Case investigation forms. Simplified syndrome-based reporting (14 priority syndromes).
profile.users_scale: Active in 8+ crisis-affected countries. Hundreds of sentinel health facilities report. Covers millions of displaced and conflict-affected populations.
profile.access_model: WHO emergency program. Data shared with national health authorities and humanitarian partners. Weekly bulletins published. Not publicly accessible at facility level.

all.d: State-level electronic disease surveillance system for case management, investigation, and reporting of notifiable conditions in US public health jurisdictions

profile.overview: EpiTrax is a state-level electronic disease surveillance system for case management, disease investigation, and reporting used by multiple US states for notifiable disease tracking.
profile.functional_scope: Electronic disease surveillance: case reporting, investigation management, contact tracing, outbreak management, laboratory result integration, and reporting to CDC NNDSS. Workflow management for disease investigators.
profile.tech_stack: Web-based application. Ruby on Rails. PostgreSQL database. HL7 electronic lab reporting (ELR) integration. FHIR compatibility. Open-source.
profile.operator: Utah Department of Health (original developer). Used by multiple US states. Community-maintained open-source project.
profile.data_model: Case-level surveillance records with demographics, symptoms, lab results, exposures, travel history. Investigation workflows. Compliant with NNDSS MMG specifications.
profile.users_scale: Deployed in multiple US states serving millions of residents. Processes thousands of disease investigations annually per state.
profile.access_model: Open-source software. Free for state/local health departments. Technical implementation support. Data access restricted to authorized epidemiologists.

all.d: Saudi national antimicrobial resistance monitoring program aligned with WHO GLASS

profile.overview: Saudi Arabia's national antimicrobial resistance surveillance program monitors AMR patterns across the country, contributing to WHO GLASS and regional AMR intelligence.
profile.functional_scope: National AMR surveillance: monitors resistance patterns in key bacterial pathogens across Saudi healthcare facilities, provides antibiogram data, informs antimicrobial stewardship programs, and reports to WHO GLASS.
profile.tech_stack: Laboratory information systems integration. WHONET software for AMR data management. Electronic reporting to national AMR database.
profile.operator: Saudi Ministry of Health (MOH), National AMR Committee. WHO GLASS participating country. Saudi CDC coordination.
profile.data_model: Antimicrobial susceptibility testing (AST) results, organism identification, specimen types, facility data. WHONET-compatible format. GLASS reporting structure.
profile.users_scale: Covers major Saudi healthcare facilities. Data from thousands of clinical isolates annually. Reports to WHO GLASS.
profile.access_model: Government surveillance program. Data accessible to authorized MOH personnel. Aggregate national reports published. GLASS data publicly available.

all.d: French Direction Générale de l'Armement NRBC division providing biological threat detection and CBRN defense capabilities to French armed forces

profile.overview: The French Direction Générale de l'Armement (DGA) NRBC (Nuclear, Radiological, Biological, Chemical) division manages France's military CBRN defense research, development, and procurement programs.
profile.functional_scope: French military CBRN defense: develops and procures detection systems, protective equipment, decontamination technologies, and medical countermeasures. Manages DGA Maîtrise NRBC test center at Vert-le-Petit.
profile.tech_stack: CBRN detection and identification systems, protective equipment, decontamination platforms. DGA Maîtrise NRBC test and evaluation facilities.
profile.operator: Direction Générale de l'Armement (DGA), French Ministry of Armed Forces. DGA Maîtrise NRBC center at Vert-le-Petit, Essonne.
profile.data_model: Defense acquisition program data, test and evaluation results, threat characterization reports. Classified military intelligence.
profile.users_scale: Serves French Armed Forces (200,000+ active). Supports NATO and EU CBRN operations. Coordinates with UK Dstl, German WIS.
profile.access_model: French military program. Classified operations. Some published research. International cooperation through NATO and EU frameworks.

all.d: China Integrated Surveillance of Disease Control and Prevention - nationwide near-real-time infectious disease reporting system covering 1.4B population

profile.overview: The China Information System for Disease Control and Prevention (CISDCP) is China's national electronic disease surveillance platform operated by China CDC, covering all healthcare facilities for real-time notifiable disease reporting.
profile.functional_scope: National electronic disease surveillance: real-time reporting of 40+ notifiable diseases from all healthcare facilities, automated outbreak detection, sentinel surveillance for influenza and other pathogens, and public health emergency reporting.
profile.tech_stack: Web-based reporting system accessible from all hospitals/clinics. Real-time data transmission. GIS mapping. Automated aberration detection algorithms. National data center at China CDC.
profile.operator: Chinese Center for Disease Control and Prevention (China CDC), Beijing, China. Ministry of Health oversight.
profile.data_model: Case-level data for notifiable diseases: demographics, diagnosis, lab results, onset date, geographic location. Real-time transmission within 24 hours. National aggregate statistics.
profile.users_scale: Covers China's 1.4 billion population. All healthcare facilities report mandatorily. Processes millions of disease reports annually. World's largest infectious disease surveillance system.
profile.access_model: Government system. Accessible to authorized health personnel at all administrative levels. Aggregate data published in Chinese CDC weekly reports. Limited international data sharing.

all.d: IARPA Biointelligence & Biosecurity Innovation Center - developing methods to detect, collect, analyze & prevent biological threats including AI-bio risks

profile.overview: IARPA B24IC (Biointelligence & Biosecurity Innovation Center) develops advanced analytical tools and technologies for the US intelligence community to detect and assess biological threats globally.
profile.functional_scope: Intelligence-focused biosecurity analytics: develops novel methods for detecting covert biological programs, assessing biothreat capabilities, monitoring dual-use research, and providing biological intelligence analysis support.
profile.tech_stack: AI/ML for biological intelligence analysis, open-source intelligence (OSINT) tools, satellite imagery analysis, scientific literature mining, synthetic biology monitoring.
profile.operator: Intelligence Advanced Research Projects Activity (IARPA), Office of the Director of National Intelligence (ODNI), Washington, DC, USA.
profile.data_model: Classified intelligence products. OSINT analysis databases. Satellite imagery, scientific publication monitoring, biotechnology capability assessments.
profile.users_scale: Serves US intelligence community: CIA, DIA, NSA, and other agencies. Informs National Biodefense Strategy assessments.
profile.access_model: Classified intelligence program. Access restricted to cleared IC personnel. Research performed under classified contracts.

all.d: Finnish military-grade lab-in-a-box biodefense immunochromatographic test system for 7 biological warfare agents including anthrax, ricin, botulinum

profile.overview: The ENVI Assay System by Bertin Environics (Environics Oy, Finland) is a military-grade lab-in-a-box immunochromatographic biological agent detection system for field use by military and first responders.
profile.functional_scope: Field-deployable biological agent identification: immunochromatographic assay system for rapid identification of biological warfare agents and toxins. Portable, battery-operated system for use in contaminated environments.
profile.tech_stack: Lateral flow immunoassay (LFI) technology, portable reader unit, assay cartridges for multiple agents, ruggedized case for field deployment.
profile.operator: Bertin Environics Oy (subsidiary of Bertin Technologies/CNIM Group), Mikkeli, Finland. NATO-country military customers.
profile.data_model: Assay results with agent identification, sample metadata, reader measurements. Exportable to C2 systems.
profile.users_scale: Deployed by Finnish, NATO, and allied military forces. Used in CBRN exercises and real-world operations.
profile.access_model: Military/government procurement. Export controlled. Assay cartridges sold separately.

all.d: WHO's field-deployable tool for outbreak investigation, including case management, contact tracing, and transmission chain visualization

profile.overview: Go.Data is WHO's free, open-source field-deployable outbreak investigation tool for case management, contact tracing, and visualization of transmission chains during disease outbreaks.
profile.functional_scope: Outbreak investigation: case registration and management, contact tracing and follow-up, laboratory result tracking, transmission chain visualization, GIS mapping, and data export. Supports Ebola, COVID-19, Mpox, and other outbreaks.
profile.tech_stack: Node.js backend with LoopBack framework. Angular frontend. MongoDB database. Works offline (mobile/tablet). REST API. Docker deployment. Interoperability with DHIS2, Epi Info.
profile.operator: WHO Health Emergencies Programme (WHE). Open-source with community contributions. Supported by WHO and GOARN.
profile.data_model: Case records (demographics, symptoms, lab results), contact records, exposure events, follow-up data. Transmission trees. GIS coordinates. FHIR-compatible data exchange.
profile.users_scale: Deployed in 100+ countries. Used during COVID-19 by dozens of national health ministries. Thousands of trained users. 50,000+ person data capacity.
profile.access_model: Free and open-source. Downloadable from who.int/godata. Cloud or local deployment. Training materials freely available.

all.d: Korea Institute for Defense Analyses biosecurity and CBRN threat assessment division providing policy analysis and defense capability evaluation for ROK military biodefense

profile.overview: The Korea Institute for Defense Analyses (KIDA) provides biosecurity and CBRN threat analysis for the Republic of Korea military, supporting defense policy and capability development.
profile.functional_scope: Korean defense biosecurity analysis: CBRN threat assessments, biodefense capability evaluation, defense policy analysis, military medical preparedness, and inter-Korean biological threat analysis.
profile.tech_stack: Policy analysis frameworks, wargaming/simulation tools, threat modeling, intelligence analysis methodologies.
profile.operator: Korea Institute for Defense Analyses (KIDA), Seoul, South Korea. Think tank under the Ministry of National Defense.
profile.data_model: Defense policy analysis reports, CBRN threat assessments, capability gap analyses, simulation results. Classified and unclassified products.
profile.users_scale: Serves ROK Ministry of National Defense and military leadership. Informs Korean defense budget and capability decisions.
profile.access_model: Government defense institute. Reports for authorized Korean government/military officials. Some unclassified publications available.

all.d: FAO's Global Animal Disease Information System for real-time monitoring and early warning of transboundary animal diseases and zoonotic threats

profile.overview: EMPRES-i (Emergency Prevention System for Transboundary Animal and Plant Pests and Diseases) is FAO's global animal disease information system providing real-time alerts and tracking for transboundary animal diseases.
profile.functional_scope: Global animal disease surveillance: real-time event tracking, disease outbreak mapping, transboundary disease alerts, avian influenza monitoring, foot-and-mouth disease tracking, and One Health event analysis. Integrated with GLEWS+.
profile.tech_stack: Web-based GIS platform. Interactive disease maps. Real-time data feeds from WOAH, FAO country offices, and national veterinary services. API for data access.
profile.operator: Food and Agriculture Organization of the United Nations (FAO), Animal Health Service, Rome, Italy.
profile.data_model: Animal disease events with species, location, date, diagnosis, source. GIS-referenced. Time series of disease incidence. Integration with WAHIS/WOAH data.
profile.users_scale: Global coverage. Used by national veterinary services, WHO, WOAH, and animal health researchers worldwide. Free public access.
profile.access_model: Free public access at empres-i.fao.org. Interactive maps and data downloads. No registration required for basic access. API available.

all.d: CDC/APHIS regulatory program overseeing possession, use and transfer of 67 select biological agents and toxins with security risk potential

profile.overview: The US Federal Select Agent Program (FSAP) regulates the possession, use, and transfer of biological select agents and toxins that could pose a severe threat to public health, agriculture, or environment.
profile.functional_scope: Regulatory oversight of 67 select agents/toxins: registration of entities, personnel reliability program (suitability assessments), inspections, transfer approvals, incident reporting, and enforcement. Dual oversight by CDC/DSAT and USDA/APHIS.
profile.tech_stack: Electronic registration system (eRIDS). Secure database of registered entities and responsible officials. Inspection management system. Incident tracking database.
profile.operator: CDC Division of Select Agents and Toxins (DSAT) and USDA Animal and Plant Health Inspection Service (APHIS). Joint regulatory oversight.
profile.data_model: Entity registrations, personnel records, agent inventories, inspection findings, theft/loss/release reports. 42 CFR Part 73 compliance data.
profile.users_scale: Oversees ~600 registered entities and ~12,000 approved individuals in the US. Covers research universities, government labs, pharmaceutical companies, and commercial entities.
profile.access_model: Regulatory program. Registration database confidential. FSAP oversight information and guidance at selectagents.gov. Public annual reports. Entity list not publicly disclosed for security.

all.d: Joint US Forces Korea Portal and Integrated Threat Recognition - portable biological detection and biosurveillance network at Korean military installations
