profile.overview: JUPITR (Joint US Forces Korea Portal and Integrated Threat Recognition) is a biosurveillance system for US Forces Korea providing integrated biological threat monitoring on the Korean Peninsula.
profile.functional_scope: Integrated biosurveillance for Korean Peninsula: environmental biological monitoring, syndromic surveillance of military/civilian populations, threat alert dissemination, and coordinated response between USFK and Korean health authorities.
profile.tech_stack: Sensor integration, electronic health record surveillance, environmental monitoring, GIS-based threat visualization, integration with Korean biosurveillance systems.
profile.operator: US Forces Korea (USFK) public health command. Integration with KDCA and Joint ROK-US biosurveillance infrastructure.
profile.data_model: Environmental monitoring data, syndromic surveillance signals, biological detection alerts, health threat assessments. Integrated multi-source intelligence.
profile.users_scale: Covers USFK installations and surrounding areas. Serves force health protection for 28,500+ US troops and associated personnel.
profile.access_model: Military system. Restricted to authorized USFK and ROK military personnel. Not publicly accessible.

all.d: CDC's free epidemiological analysis toolkit providing questionnaire design, data entry, statistical analysis, and mapping for field epidemiology

profile.overview: Epi Info is CDC's free epidemiological analysis software providing questionnaire design, data entry, and statistical analysis for outbreak investigations and public health surveillance. In use since 1985.
profile.functional_scope: Epidemiological toolkit: customizable questionnaire design, data entry and management, statistical analysis (frequencies, 2x2 tables, logistic regression), GIS mapping, line listing, and epidemic curve generation. Offline-capable.
profile.tech_stack: Windows desktop application (C#/.NET). Access/SQL database backend. Map module with GIS. Mobile companion (Epi Info for Android). Open-source on GitHub.
profile.operator: CDC Division of Health Informatics and Surveillance, Atlanta, GA, USA. Maintained by CDC informatics staff.
profile.data_model: Customizable survey/case report forms. Relational database storage. Import/export: CSV, Access, Excel, XML. Statistical output tables.
profile.users_scale: Used by public health professionals in 180+ countries. Millions of downloads. Standard field epidemiology tool. WHO and FETP training programs worldwide.
profile.access_model: Completely free. Download from cdc.gov/epiinfo. No registration. Open-source on GitHub. Multilingual documentation. Training available.

all.d: DHS nationwide air-sampling network in 30+ US cities for early detection of aerosolized biological attacks

profile.overview: BioWatch is the DHS nationwide environmental bioaerosol monitoring network deployed in 30+ US cities, providing continuous air sampling for early detection of biological terrorism agents.
profile.functional_scope: Nationwide biological terrorism detection: continuous outdoor air sampling, laboratory analysis for select biological warfare agents (anthrax, plague, tularemia, smallpox, etc.), alerting to federal/state/local authorities, and integration with public health response.
profile.tech_stack: Autonomous air sampling units (dry filter technology). Samples analyzed at Laboratory Response Network (LRN) labs using real-time PCR. BioWatch Advisory Committee for decision support.
profile.operator: Department of Homeland Security, Office of Health Security. Operational 24/7 in coordination with EPA, CDC, FBI, and local first responders.
profile.data_model: Air sample collection metadata, PCR analysis results, BioWatch Actionable Result (BAR) notifications, environmental data (weather, location). Classified detection algorithms.
profile.users_scale: Deployed in 30+ major US metropolitan areas. Covers hundreds of millions of urban residents. DHS's primary environmental biodetection program.
profile.access_model: Government classified program. Specific deployment locations not publicly disclosed. Congressional oversight. Alert protocols involve multiple federal agencies.

all.d: KAUST Pathogen Genomics Lab tools including AssayM for RT-PCR primer surveillance

profile.overview: KAUST (King Abdullah University of Science and Technology) Pathogen Genomics Lab (PGL) develops genomic tools including AssayM for automated design and validation of RT-PCR assays for pathogen surveillance.
profile.functional_scope: Pathogen genomics research and tool development: AssayM software for automated RT-PCR assay design and in silico validation, SARS-CoV-2 genomic surveillance, metagenomic analysis, and capacity building for Middle East region.
profile.tech_stack: Python bioinformatics tools (AssayM). Illumina/Nanopore sequencing. Custom analysis pipelines. Cloud computing. Published on GitHub.
profile.operator: King Abdullah University of Science and Technology (KAUST), Thuwal, Saudi Arabia. Funded by KAUST.
profile.data_model: Pathogen genome sequences, PCR primer/probe designs, in silico validation results, surveillance metadata. Submissions to GISAID/NCBI.
profile.users_scale: Regional genomic surveillance capacity. Published research. Tools used by Middle East public health labs. International collaborations.
profile.access_model: Open-source tools on GitHub. Research publications freely available. Academic collaborations. KAUST institutional access for internal resources.

st: Global coverage spanning multiple continents and regions
wk: Reporting delays between event occurrence and data availability
all.d: World Organisation for Animal Health's global information system for official reporting of animal diseases, providing real-time alerts and historical data across 182 member countries

profile.overview: WAHIS (World Animal Health Information System) is the World Organisation for Animal Health's (WOAH, formerly OIE) global information system for animal disease reporting, serving as the official source for animal health status worldwide.
profile.functional_scope: Global animal disease surveillance: immediate notification of 117 listed diseases, 6-monthly reports, annual reports on disease status, antimicrobial use data, wildlife disease monitoring. Official source for international trade health status.
profile.tech_stack: Web-based reporting platform. Interactive dashboards and maps. API for data access. Integration with national veterinary information systems. GIS visualization.
profile.operator: World Organisation for Animal Health (WOAH), Paris, France. 183 member countries and territories report mandatorily.
profile.data_model: Disease events with species, location, date, measures taken. Quantitative data on cases, deaths, vaccinations. Official disease freedom declarations. Standardized WOAH codes.
profile.users_scale: 183 member countries/territories report. Used by FAO, WHO, importers/exporters, veterinary services, and researchers. Official basis for international trade decisions.
profile.access_model: Free public access at wahis.woah.org. Interactive maps and data downloads. No registration for basic access. API available.

all.d: DRDO Defence Food Research Laboratory and Centre for Fire, Explosive & Environment Safety providing biological agent detection and BSL-4 capabilities

profile.overview: India's Defence Research and Development Organisation (DRDO) conducts biodefense research through its Defence Food Research Laboratory (DFRL) in Mysore and Centre for Fire, Explosive, and Environment Safety (CFEES) in Delhi.
profile.functional_scope: Indian military biodefense: biological agent detection and decontamination, NBC protection systems, biosafety equipment, food safety for armed forces, environmental monitoring, and CBRN defense technology development.
profile.tech_stack: Biological detection systems, NBC filtration and protection equipment, decontamination technologies, portable field laboratories. Integration with Indian military C2.
profile.operator: Defence Research and Development Organisation (DRDO), Ministry of Defence, India. DFRL (Mysore) and CFEES (Delhi) are primary labs.
profile.data_model: Defense research data, biological threat characterization, equipment specifications, field trial results. Mostly classified.
profile.users_scale: Serves Indian Armed Forces (1.4M+ active personnel). India's primary military biodefense capability. Coordinates with ICMR for dual-use research.
profile.access_model: Government defense organization. Classified research programs. Some publications in DRDO journals and peer-reviewed literature.

all.d: FDA's distributed network of laboratories performing whole-genome sequencing of foodborne pathogens for rapid identification and outbreak source tracking

profile.overview: GenomeTrakr is the FDA's distributed network of state and federal laboratories performing whole-genome sequencing of foodborne pathogens for surveillance and outbreak detection, integrated with NCBI Pathogen Detection.
profile.functional_scope: Foodborne pathogen genomic surveillance: WGS of Salmonella, Listeria, E. coli, Campylobacter, Vibrio, and Cronobacter isolates. Real-time sequence sharing through NCBI. Cluster detection for outbreak identification. Source tracking.
profile.tech_stack: Illumina whole-genome sequencing. NCBI Pathogen Detection pipeline for automated clustering. BaseSpace for sequencing. BioNumerics for analysis. Sequences deposited in NCBI SRA.
profile.operator: FDA Center for Food Safety and Applied Nutrition (CFSAN). Partnership with USDA FSIS, CDC PulseNet, and state public health laboratories.
profile.data_model: Whole-genome sequences with structured metadata (organism, source, location, date, isolation source). NCBI BioSample/SRA format. SNP clusters for outbreak detection.
profile.users_scale: 25+ participating laboratories (FDA, state, international). 650,000+ pathogen genomes sequenced. Integrated with PulseNet for US foodborne disease outbreak detection.
profile.access_model: Sequence data publicly available in NCBI SRA. GenomeTrakr network data freely accessible. Open collaboration for laboratories to join the network.

all.c: Next-Gen Epidemic Intelligence from Open Sources
all.d: WHO upgraded Epidemic Intelligence from Open Sources platform with enhanced AI/ML capabilities for automated event detection across news, social media and official reports in 60+ languages

profile.overview: EIOS (Epidemic Intelligence from Open Sources) is WHO's AI/ML-enhanced epidemic intelligence platform for early detection of public health events. It monitors and analyzes information from diverse open sources including news media, social media, and official reports in multiple languages.
profile.functional_scope: Automated web crawling and aggregation from 150,000+ sources, natural language processing in 50+ languages, event-based surveillance signal detection and categorization, risk assessment and alert generation, geo-tagging and mapping, trend analysis and dashboards, collaborative investigation workflows.
profile.tech_stack: AI/ML pipeline: NLP (entity extraction, sentiment analysis, topic modeling), machine learning classifiers for disease signal detection, web crawling infrastructure. Cloud-hosted with scalable architecture. Custom dashboards and API.
profile.operator: World Health Organization (WHO), in collaboration with the Joint Research Centre (JRC) of the European Commission and other partners in the Epidemic Intelligence Community.
profile.data_model: Structured events: disease, date, location, source, affected population, signal type, verification status. Multi-language text corpus with entity annotations.
profile.users_scale: Used by WHO and 100+ national public health agencies. Monitors 150,000+ sources globally. Processes millions of articles daily.
profile.access_model: Access for WHO and partner public health agencies. Not publicly available. Restricted to authorized users in the Epidemic Intelligence Community.

st: Aligned with OpenHIE architecture for health information exchange
all.d: Open Health Information Mediator providing an interoperability layer for health information exchange between disparate health systems using OpenHIE architecture

profile.overview: OpenHIM (Open Health Information Mediator) is an open-source middleware platform providing an interoperability layer for health information exchange, connecting disparate health systems in low-resource settings.
profile.functional_scope: Health information exchange middleware: message routing, transformation (HL7v2, FHIR, CDA), security/access control, audit logging, and transaction management. Connects laboratories, hospitals, registries, and surveillance systems.
profile.tech_stack: Node.js backend (OpenHIM Core). React admin console. MongoDB. HL7v2, FHIR R4, CSD, ADX standards. Docker deployment. OpenAPI. Open-source on GitHub (jembi/openhim-core-js).
profile.operator: Jembi Health Systems (South Africa). Open-source community. Part of OpenHIE (Open Health Information Exchange) architecture.
profile.data_model: Health message transactions with routing rules, transformations, and audit trails. Mediator architecture for protocol adaptation. Channel-based message filtering.
profile.users_scale: Deployed in 10+ African countries. Core component of Rwanda HIS, South Africa NDoH systems. Used by WHO, PEPFAR, and Global Fund projects.
profile.access_model: Free and open-source (MPL 2.0). Available on GitHub. Community support. Implementation consulting available from Jembi.

all.c: AI Epidemic Intelligence as a Service
all.d: Columbia University health tech startup delivering epidemic intelligence using ML, AI, and geospatial analytics for early detection and prediction

profile.overview: Santé is a Columbia University health technology startup delivering AI-powered epidemic intelligence as a service for public health decision-making.
profile.functional_scope: AI epidemic intelligence: automated disease trend analysis, outbreak risk prediction, integrated epidemiological-genomic analytics, and decision support for public health authorities.
profile.tech_stack: AI/ML analytics platform. Cloud-based SaaS. Integration with multiple data sources. Web dashboard for visualization.
profile.operator: Santé Health Tech, affiliated with Columbia University, New York, NY, USA.
profile.data_model: Multi-source epidemiological data integration. AI-generated risk scores and predictions. Dashboard visualizations.
profile.users_scale: Emerging platform serving public health organizations. Academic research foundation from Columbia University.
profile.access_model: Commercial SaaS model. Partnerships with public health agencies. Academic research component.

all.d: State Research Center of Virology and Biotechnology VECTOR in Novosibirsk - one of two WHO-authorized smallpox repositories and Russian biodefense backbone

profile.overview: The State Research Center of Virology and Biotechnology VECTOR is one of Russia's largest virology research institutes, located in Koltsovo, Novosibirsk Oblast. One of only two WHO-authorized repositories for variola (smallpox) virus.
profile.functional_scope: Virology research and biodefense: studies highly dangerous pathogens (variola, Ebola, Marburg), vaccine development (EpiVacCorona COVID-19 vaccine), diagnostic development, and biological defense research. BSL-4 facility.
profile.tech_stack: BSL-4 maximum containment facility. Advanced virology, molecular biology, genomic sequencing, vaccine production capabilities. Animal testing facilities.
profile.operator: Russian Federal Service for Surveillance on Consumer Rights Protection (Rospotrebnadzor). Under Russian government oversight. ~7,000 employees.
profile.data_model: Classified pathogen research data. Published research in Russian and international journals. WHO variola virus advisory committee oversight.
profile.users_scale: Major Russian biodefense institute. Serves Russian Armed Forces and civilian health agencies. International collaboration through WHO (variola oversight).
profile.access_model: Russian government facility. Mostly classified. Some publications in peer-reviewed journals. WHO oversight for variola repository. Limited international access.

all.d: R package for automated generation of epidemiological situation reports, developed with MSF and WHO templates for outbreak response documentation

profile.overview: Sitrep is an R package from the R4Epid project providing templates for automated generation of epidemiological situation reports during outbreak response.
profile.functional_scope: Automated situation report generation: standardized templates for outbreak reports, descriptive epidemiology tables, epidemic curves, attack rate calculations, and mortality summaries. Designed for field epidemiologists.
profile.tech_stack: R package using R Markdown templates. Integrates with dplyr, ggplot2, flextable. Produces Word/HTML/PDF reports. Open-source on GitHub (R4EPI/sitrep).
profile.operator: R4EPI project. Community-developed open-source. Supported by MSF (Doctors Without Borders) Epicentre.
profile.data_model: Line list input data (CSV/Excel). Standardized output: summary tables, epidemic curves, geographic distribution maps, demographic breakdowns.
profile.users_scale: Used by MSF, WHO GOARN, and field epidemiologists during outbreaks. Training tool for Field Epidemiology Training Programs.
profile.access_model: Free and open-source (MIT license). Available on GitHub. Documentation and tutorials. Training materials for epidemic response.

all.d: Clinical surveillance platform integrating electronic health record data with public health reporting for real-time monitoring of disease trends in healthcare settings

profile.overview: MedStar Health is a large nonprofit healthcare system in the Washington DC-Baltimore area whose surveillance capabilities integrate clinical data for syndromic and disease surveillance purposes.
profile.functional_scope: Clinical surveillance: electronic health record (EHR)-based syndromic surveillance, emergency department chief complaint monitoring, laboratory reporting integration, and contribution to regional surveillance networks.
profile.tech_stack: Epic/Cerner EHR integration. Automated syndromic surveillance feeds. Integration with BioSense Platform. HL7/FHIR messaging.
profile.operator: MedStar Health system, Washington DC and Maryland, USA. 10 hospitals, 300+ outpatient sites.
profile.data_model: Syndromic surveillance data from emergency departments and clinics. Chief complaints, diagnosis codes, lab results. De-identified data shared with public health.
profile.users_scale: Serves 4M+ patients in DC-Baltimore region. Contributes to DC Department of Health and Maryland DHMH surveillance.
profile.access_model: Clinical system. De-identified data shared with public health authorities per regulatory requirements. Not publicly accessible.

wk: Geographically limited deployment covering only specific regional areas
all.d: Electronic Integrated Disease Surveillance System supporting One Health surveillance of both human and animal diseases in Central Asian and Caucasus countries

profile.overview: EIDSS (Electronic Integrated Disease Surveillance System) is a comprehensive surveillance system supporting disease monitoring, sample tracking, and outbreak investigation across multiple countries, particularly in the former Soviet region.
profile.functional_scope: Integrated disease surveillance: human and veterinary disease reporting, laboratory sample management, outbreak investigation, GIS mapping, and antimicrobial resistance monitoring. Bilingual/multilingual support.
profile.tech_stack: Web-based .NET application. SQL Server database. LIMS integration. GIS module. Mobile data collection. HL7 interoperability. DTRA-funded development.
profile.operator: Developed under DTRA Biological Threat Reduction Program. Deployed by national health authorities in partner countries (Georgia, Azerbaijan, Ukraine, Kazakhstan, etc.).
profile.data_model: Human/veterinary disease cases, laboratory samples, outbreak events, geographic data. Customizable data entry forms per national requirements.
profile.users_scale: Deployed in 8+ former Soviet countries. Used by national public health and veterinary services. Thousands of users across deployed countries.
profile.access_model: Government surveillance system in deploying countries. Not publicly accessible. Technical support through DTRA cooperative agreements.

profile.overview: CanPath (Canadian Partnership for Tomorrow's Health) is Canada's largest population health cohort platform, following 330,000+ participants across 7 regional cohorts (BC Generations Project, Alberta's Tomorrow Project, Manitoba Tomorrow Project, Ontario Health Study, CARTaGENE, Atlantic PATH, Healthy Future Sask). Launched in 2009, it integrates questionnaire data, biological samples (blood, urine, saliva), and linked administrative health records. In 2024, CanPath partnered with Lifebit and AWS to launch a cloud-based Trusted Research Environment (TRE) for secure, ethical access to anonymized health data. CanPath is expanding its genomic data landscape with genotyping arrays and whole-genome sequencing from Ontario and Quebec cohorts.
profile.functional_scope: Large-scale population health research: longitudinal cohort tracking, biobank management (blood, urine, saliva), genomic data access (SNP arrays, WGS), environmental exposure assessment, lifestyle and behavioral data collection, linked health administrative records, and secure research data access via Trusted Research Environment.
profile.tech_stack: AWS cloud-based Trusted Research Environment (Lifebit platform), REDCap for data collection, Illumina genotyping arrays, whole-genome sequencing pipelines, secure CanPath Access Portal for researcher queries, OHDSI/OMOP common data model for harmonization.
profile.operator: CanPath National Office (hosted at Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, OICR). Funded by Canadian Partnership Against Cancer, provincial health authorities, and Genome Canada. Member of NCI Cohort Consortium.
profile.data_model: Harmonized cohort data covering demographics, health history, lifestyle, environmental exposures, biological samples, and genomic data. Over 1 billion data points across 330,000+ participants. Schema.org-compatible metadata.
profile.users_scale: 330,000+ enrolled participants, 7 provincial cohorts, hundreds of active research projects. Data accessible to qualified Canadian and international researchers.
profile.access_model: Researcher access via CanPath Access Portal (application-based, requires ethics approval). Cloud-based TRE for in-situ analysis. Biological samples available for approved studies. Free for academic researchers.

all.d: Canada's largest population cohort platform following 330,000+ participants across 7 regional cohorts, integrating questionnaire, biobank, and genomic data

wk: Dated user interface with limited modern visualization capabilities
all.d: Statistical software for spatial, temporal, and space-time cluster detection using scan statistics, widely used in disease surveillance and outbreak investigation

profile.overview: SatScan is a free statistical software for spatial, temporal, and space-time cluster detection developed by Martin Kulldorff (Harvard/Brigham and Women's Hospital). The most widely used spatial cluster detection tool in epidemiology.
profile.functional_scope: Spatial and temporal cluster detection: scan statistics for identifying disease clusters, prospective surveillance for early outbreak detection, purely temporal, purely spatial, and space-time analyses. Supports Poisson, Bernoulli, and other models.
profile.tech_stack: Standalone Windows/Mac application (Java). Command-line interface. Input: CSV case/population files. Output: clusters with p-values, relative risks, and geographic coordinates. Integration with GIS software.
profile.operator: Martin Kulldorff (currently at University of Denver). Previously Harvard Medical School. Free distribution.
profile.data_model: Input: case locations (coordinates/geo-IDs), population denominators, time periods. Output: detected clusters with statistical significance, relative risk, and geographic boundaries.
profile.users_scale: Downloaded by 100,000+ users. Cited in 5,000+ publications. Used by CDC, WHO, state health departments, and epidemiologists worldwide. Standard tool for cluster detection.
profile.access_model: Completely free. Download from satscan.org. No registration. Cross-platform (Windows/Mac). No restrictions.

all.n: China AMMS (Academy of Military Medical Sciences)
all.d: PLA Academy of Military Medical Sciences - China's premier military biodefense institution under Central Military Commission with dual-use capabilities

profile.overview: The PLA Academy of Military Medical Sciences (AMMS) is China's premier military medical research institution, conducting biodefense research, vaccine development, and military medical science. Part of the PLA Academy of Military Science since 2017.
profile.functional_scope: Chinese military medical and biodefense research: vaccine development (Ad5-nCoV COVID-19 vaccine by Chen Wei), biological agent defense, medical countermeasures, military medical AI, CBRN protection, and biosafety research.
profile.tech_stack: BSL-4 laboratory (Wuhan Institute of Virology collaboration). Advanced molecular biology, immunology, genomics. Vaccine manufacturing capabilities.
profile.operator: PLA Academy of Military Science, Beijing, China. Under Central Military Commission. Multiple subordinate institutes.
profile.data_model: Classified military medical research data. Published research in Chinese and international journals. Patent filings.
profile.users_scale: Serves PLA (2M+ active personnel) and Chinese national defense. Major contributor to Chinese COVID-19 vaccine development. International publications.
profile.access_model: Chinese military institution. Most research classified. Some publications in open literature. Limited international collaboration.

st: Extensible plugin architecture supporting custom analytical modules
all.d: Open-source geographic information system adapted for health applications, providing spatial analysis, mapping, and visualization of epidemiological data

profile.overview: QGIS is the leading open-source geographic information system software, widely adapted for spatial epidemiology, disease mapping, and public health surveillance applications.
profile.functional_scope: Spatial epidemiology and disease mapping: point pattern analysis, disease rate mapping, health facility accessibility, vector habitat mapping, outbreak cluster visualization, and environmental health analysis. Plugin ecosystem for health applications.
profile.tech_stack: C++/Python application. Cross-platform (Windows/Mac/Linux). 1,800+ plugins. Python API (PyQGIS). Integration with R, PostGIS, web mapping. Processing framework.
profile.operator: QGIS.org community project. Open Source Geospatial Foundation (OSGeo). Global volunteer developer community.
profile.data_model: Vector (shapefile, GeoJSON) and raster (GeoTIFF) spatial data. Database connections (PostGIS, SpatiaLite). Web services (WMS, WFS). Health-specific: disease incidence layers, facility locations, population surfaces.
profile.users_scale: Millions of users globally. Standard GIS tool in public health programs worldwide. Used by WHO, CDC, MSF, and hundreds of health ministries.
profile.access_model: Free and open-source (GPL-2.0). Download from qgis.org. No registration. Community support. Training materials freely available.

all.d: WHO's global web-based tool for influenza virological surveillance, collecting data from National Influenza Centres in the Global Influenza Surveillance Network since 1997

profile.overview: FluNet is WHO's global web-based tool for influenza virological surveillance, providing data on influenza virus types, subtypes, and lineages reported by 150+ National Influenza Centres worldwide.
profile.functional_scope: Global influenza virological surveillance: reports influenza type (A/B), subtype (H1N1, H3N2), lineage (Victoria/Yamagata), from sentinel and non-sentinel sources. Interactive charts and maps. Seasonal and zoonotic influenza monitoring.
profile.tech_stack: Web-based platform. Interactive data visualization. API for data download. Integration with FluID (epidemiological data) and GISRS network.
profile.operator: WHO Global Influenza Programme, Geneva, Switzerland. Data from Global Influenza Surveillance and Response System (GISRS) with 150+ NICs.
profile.data_model: Weekly virological data: specimens processed, influenza positive counts by type/subtype, sentinel vs non-sentinel. Country-level reporting. Historical data from 1997.
profile.users_scale: 150+ countries report. Used by WHO for vaccine strain selection. Referenced by national immunization programs, researchers, and media worldwide.
profile.access_model: Free public access at who.int/tools/flunet. Interactive dashboards. Data downloads (CSV). No registration. WHO FluMart for bulk data access.

all.d: WHO's Epidemic Intelligence from Open Sources platform for automated detection, filtering, and assessment of public health events from digital media worldwide

profile.overview: EIOS (Epidemic Intelligence from Open Sources) is WHO's AI-powered platform for automated detection and assessment of public health events from open-source information, serving as an evolution of earlier event-based surveillance systems.
profile.functional_scope: Automated epidemic intelligence: AI-powered scanning of global media, social media, official reports, and scientific publications for disease signals. Multi-language NLP. Automated event categorization and risk assessment. Collaborative analysis workspace.
profile.tech_stack: AI/NLP for multilingual text mining and event extraction. Cloud-based analytics. Collaborative web platform. Integration with WHO IHR event management.
profile.operator: WHO Health Emergencies Programme. Partnership with Joint Research Centre (JRC) of the European Commission. Global network of users.
profile.data_model: Disease events extracted from open sources with disease, location, date, source URL, and severity indicators. Collaborative signal assessment. Verified event records.
profile.users_scale: Used by WHO, ECDC, national public health agencies in 50+ countries. Processes thousands of signals daily. Global collaborative network.
profile.access_model: Available to WHO and partner public health agencies. Registration through WHO. Not publicly accessible. Training provided to national focal points.

wk: Coverage limited to EU/EEA member states only
all.d: European Centre for Disease Prevention and Control's central surveillance platform harmonizing disease reporting across EU/EEA member states with standardized case definitions

profile.overview: The European Centre for Disease Prevention and Control (ECDC) serves as the EU's central hub for disease surveillance, coordinating surveillance data from 30 EU/EEA member states through TESSy and providing risk assessments.
profile.functional_scope: EU-wide disease surveillance coordination: TESSy (The European Surveillance System) data collection for 56 diseases, rapid risk assessments, weekly surveillance reports, epidemic intelligence, Eurosurveillance journal, and technical guidance.
profile.tech_stack: TESSy web-based surveillance platform. EpiPulse for epidemic intelligence. Surveillance Atlas of Infectious Diseases (interactive maps). Publications through Eurosurveillance (peer-reviewed).
profile.operator: European Centre for Disease Prevention and Control (ECDC), Stockholm, Sweden. EU agency serving 30 EU/EEA member states.
profile.data_model: Case-based surveillance data for 56 diseases with standardized reporting formats. Aggregate data published via Surveillance Atlas. ECDC threat assessments and technical reports.
profile.users_scale: Serves 30 EU/EEA countries (~450M population). TESSy receives reports from all member states. Surveillance Atlas publicly accessible. Eurosurveillance widely read.
profile.access_model: TESSy for authorized national surveillance contacts. Aggregate data publicly available through Surveillance Atlas. All publications and risk assessments freely accessible at ecdc.europa.eu.

profile.overview: ABIS refers to the Australian Biosecurity Intelligence Network (ABIN), established with Commonwealth NCRIS (National Collaborative Research Infrastructure Strategy) funding following the 2008 identification of a National Biosecurity Framework as a top government priority. ABIN's mission is to strengthen Australia's biosecurity research, surveillance, diagnostic, and response capability by enabling researchers, industry, and government to collaborate, share data, and generate intelligence using leading-edge tools and technologies through a secure online workspace. It connects CSIRO, state/territory biosecurity agencies, and university researchers.
profile.functional_scope: Collaborative biosecurity intelligence: secure data sharing between researchers/industry/government, pathogen detection analytics, environmental monitoring integration, pest and disease surveillance coordination, diagnostic capability networking, and research collaboration infrastructure for Australia's agricultural and environmental biosecurity.
profile.tech_stack: Secure online workspace platform, CSIRO AI-enabled diagnostic tools, machine learning models for pathogen and pest detection (Object Detection Models), integration with Trellis Intelligence Platform for container screening, federated data sharing infrastructure with role-based access control.
profile.operator: Australian Biosecurity Intelligence Network (ABIN), funded by Australian Government NCRIS. Coordinated with CSIRO, Department of Agriculture, Fisheries and Forestry (DAFF), and state biosecurity agencies. Address: PO Box 225, Dickson, ACT 2602.
profile.data_model: Integrated biosecurity intelligence covering agricultural pests, animal diseases, plant pathogens, and environmental threats. Federated data model allowing cross-agency queries while maintaining data sovereignty.
profile.users_scale: Used by Australian government biosecurity agencies, CSIRO researchers, university biosecurity programs, and agricultural industry partners across all states and territories.
profile.access_model: Secure access for registered Australian biosecurity stakeholders. Requires institutional affiliation. Government-funded, no user fees for participating agencies.

all.d: Integrated biosurveillance analytics platform combining multiple data sources for comprehensive situational awareness of biological threats and disease activity

st: Free, open API with no authentication required for rapid integration

profile.overview: disease.sh is a free, open-source disease data API that served over 100 billion requests, providing programmatic access to global infectious disease statistics including COVID-19, influenza, and other pathogen data.
profile.functional_scope: RESTful API for disease statistics: global/country/state-level case counts, deaths, recoveries, vaccination data, historical time series, and geographic data. Aggregates from WHO, Johns Hopkins, Worldometer, and government sources.
profile.tech_stack: Node.js backend, Redis caching, MongoDB, hosted on cloud infrastructure. Open-source on GitHub. REST API with JSON responses.
profile.operator: Open-source community project.
profile.data_model: JSON API returning country-level and sub-national disease statistics with temporal data.
profile.users_scale: 100 billion+ API requests served. Used by thousands of applications, dashboards, and research projects.
profile.access_model: Free, open-source, no API key required. Rate limiting for fair use.

all.d: Open-source RESTful API providing real-time and historical infectious disease data aggregated from WHO, Johns Hopkins, and national health authorities
Russian MoD 48th Central Scientific Research Institute - military biological defense facility with major expansion detected by satellite imagery (2024)

profile.overview: The 48th Central Scientific Research Institute (48th TsNII) of the Russian Ministry of Defence in Sergiev Posad-6 (formerly Zagorsk-6) is Russia's primary military biological defense research institute.
profile.functional_scope: Russian military biological defense: biological agent characterization, biodefense research, medical countermeasure development, detection technologies, and decontamination. Historical involvement in Soviet biological weapons program.
profile.tech_stack: BSL-3/4 containment facilities. Military-grade biological research infrastructure. Classified research programs.
profile.operator: Russian Ministry of Defence, 48th Central Scientific Research Institute (TsNII MOD), Sergiev Posad, Moscow Oblast, Russia.
profile.data_model: Classified military biological defense data. Very limited publications in open literature.
profile.users_scale: Serves Russian Armed Forces. Key node in Russian military CBRN defense infrastructure. Subject of international nonproliferation scrutiny.
profile.access_model: Russian military classified facility. No international access. Subject of BTWC compliance concerns. NTI and other nonproliferation organizations monitor from open sources.

all.d: Africa CDC's continental surveillance platform coordinating disease monitoring and response across 55 African Union member states

profile.overview: Africa CDC's Africa Regional Event-based Surveillance (ARES) platform coordinates continental disease surveillance across 55 African Union member states, providing real-time event monitoring and response coordination.
profile.functional_scope: Continental disease surveillance: event-based surveillance across Africa, outbreak monitoring, cross-border disease intelligence, epidemic preparedness coordination, and support for national public health institutes across AU member states.
profile.tech_stack: Web-based surveillance platform. Integration with national surveillance systems. GIS mapping. Mobile reporting. Coordination with WHO AFRO.
profile.operator: Africa Centres for Disease Control and Prevention (Africa CDC), Addis Ababa, Ethiopia. African Union Commission.
profile.data_model: Disease event reports from 55 AU member states. Aggregate surveillance data. Outbreak situation reports. Regional coordination records.
profile.users_scale: Covers 55 AU member states (1.4 billion population). Coordinates with national public health institutes and Regional Collaborating Centres.
profile.access_model: AU member state access. Weekly bulletins publicly available. Not directly publicly accessible for detailed data. Aggregate reports published.

all.d: Puerto Rico's territorial public health data portal providing COVID-19 testing results, vaccination data, and disease surveillance statistics

profile.overview: BioPortal is Puerto Rico's territorial public health data portal providing disease surveillance data, COVID-19 tracking, dengue monitoring, and population health statistics for the US territory.
profile.functional_scope: Puerto Rico territorial surveillance: COVID-19 case and vaccination tracking, dengue surveillance, Zika monitoring, notifiable disease reporting, laboratory results, and vital statistics. Interactive dashboards and data downloads.
profile.tech_stack: Web-based dashboard platform. API for data access. Integration with US CDC surveillance systems. Interactive maps and charts.
profile.operator: Puerto Rico Department of Health (Departamento de Salud), San Juan, Puerto Rico. Coordination with US CDC.
profile.data_model: Disease case counts, vaccination records, laboratory results, demographic data for Puerto Rico (~3.2M population). Time series and geographic breakdowns.
profile.users_scale: Serves Puerto Rico health departments, clinicians, researchers, and public. Integration with US national surveillance systems.
profile.access_model: Free public access at bioportal.salud.pr.gov. Interactive dashboards. Downloadable datasets. No registration for public data.

wk: Command-line only tool requiring bioinformatics expertise to operate
all.d: Cluster Investigation and Virus Epidemiology Tool for genomic epidemiological investigation of SARS-CoV-2 transmission clusters using sequence and metadata

profile.overview: CIVET (Cluster Investigation and Virus Epidemiology Tool) is a bioinformatics tool from the ARTIC Network for investigating genomic clusters during pathogen outbreaks using phylogenetic context.
profile.functional_scope: Genomic cluster investigation: places outbreak sequences in broader phylogenetic context, identifies closest relatives in background datasets, generates focused phylogenetic trees, and provides cluster summaries for epidemiological interpretation.
profile.tech_stack: Python tool using Minimap2, gofasta, snipit, IQTREE2. Requires background alignment and metadata. Open-source on GitHub (artic-network/civet).
profile.operator: ARTIC Network. Developed by Áine O'Toole and Andrew Rambaut, University of Edinburgh, UK.
profile.data_model: Input: query FASTA sequences + background alignment + metadata CSV. Output: phylogenetic trees, cluster reports, timeline figures, interactive HTML reports.
profile.users_scale: Used during COVID-19 for local outbreak investigation. Popular with UK and international genomic surveillance teams. COG-UK standard tool.
profile.access_model: Free and open-source (GPL-3.0). Available on GitHub and Bioconda. Documentation and tutorials provided.

st: Long-term longitudinal surveillance spanning decades of data collection
all.d: International CJD/prion disease surveillance program based at Edinburgh University, monitoring Creutzfeldt-Jakob disease and related transmissible spongiform encephalopathies

profile.overview: International CJD/prion disease surveillance based at the National CJD Research & Surveillance Unit at the University of Edinburgh, monitoring Creutzfeldt-Jakob disease and other prion diseases globally.
profile.functional_scope: Global prion disease surveillance: monitors variant CJD (vCJD), sporadic CJD, familial CJD, and other transmissible spongiform encephalopathies. Case classification, neuropathological diagnosis, epidemiological analysis, and blood safety monitoring.
profile.tech_stack: Case reporting databases. Neuropathological analysis (Western blot, immunohistochemistry). Epidemiological surveillance. International coordination network.
profile.operator: National CJD Research & Surveillance Unit, University of Edinburgh, UK. International CJD surveillance network. WHO collaboration.
profile.data_model: Case-level CJD data: clinical features, EEG, MRI, CSF biomarkers, genetic testing, neuropathology results. International comparative databases.
profile.users_scale: Monitors CJD cases across 30+ countries. Key resource since BSE/vCJD crisis. Low case numbers but high surveillance sensitivity.
profile.access_model: Academic research unit. Surveillance reports published annually. Case data for authorized researchers. International collaboration network.

all.d: West African laboratory network strengthening diagnostic capacity and quality assurance across francophone Africa for pathogen detection and surveillance

profile.overview: RESAOLAB is a West African laboratory network strengthening diagnostic capacity across francophone West Africa, improving quality of clinical laboratory services for disease surveillance.
profile.functional_scope: Laboratory network strengthening: quality management, external quality assessment, training of laboratory technicians, supply chain management, laboratory information system deployment, and AMR surveillance support across West African laboratories.
profile.tech_stack: Laboratory quality management systems. SLIPTA (Stepwise Laboratory Improvement Process) framework. Electronic sample tracking. LIMS deployment.
profile.operator: Fondation Mérieux and partner governments (Senegal, Mali, Burkina Faso, Guinea, Niger, Togo, Benin). Funded by Agence Française de Développement (AFD).
profile.data_model: Laboratory quality indicators, EQA results, testing volumes, supply chain data. Surveillance data from participating laboratories.
profile.users_scale: Network of 700+ laboratories across 7 West African countries. Training of thousands of laboratory professionals.
profile.access_model: Institutional program. Access for participating laboratories and health ministries. Aggregate reports published. Website at resaolab.org.

all.d: Open-source electronic medical record system designed for low-resource healthcare settings, supporting disease surveillance through patient-level clinical data collection

profile.overview: OpenMRS is an open-source electronic medical record system designed for resource-constrained environments, widely deployed across sub-Saharan Africa and developing countries for patient care and disease surveillance.
profile.functional_scope: Electronic medical records: patient registration, clinical encounters, medication ordering, laboratory tracking, reporting modules, and integration with surveillance systems (DHIS2). Disease-specific modules (HIV, TB, MCH).
profile.tech_stack: Java/Spring framework. MySQL database. REST/FHIR APIs. Angular/React frontend. Mobile applications. Modular architecture with 500+ community modules. Docker deployment.
profile.operator: OpenMRS Inc. (nonprofit). Global open-source community. Key contributors include Partners in Health, Regenstrief Institute, and community developers.
profile.data_model: Patient-centric data model with concepts, encounters, observations, orders. HL7 FHIR compatible. Concept dictionary for standardized clinical coding.
profile.users_scale: Deployed in 40+ countries. 15,000+ health facilities. Manages 20M+ patient records. PEPFAR-supported HIV programs, PIH sites, and national health systems.
profile.access_model: Free and open-source (MPL 2.0). Available at openmrs.org. Community implementation support. Training materials. Cloud-hosted option (OpenMRS 3.x).

all.d: Open-source customizable dashboard template for epidemiological and genomic surveillance data visualization, adaptable for various pathogen monitoring use cases

profile.overview: Open-source customizable surveillance dashboard template providing a framework for epidemiological data visualization and situation reporting during disease outbreaks.
profile.functional_scope: Template framework for surveillance dashboards: epidemic curves, geographic maps, summary statistics, trend indicators, and comparison charts. Customizable for any disease/outbreak scenario.
profile.tech_stack: HTML/JavaScript/CSS. Chart.js or D3.js visualization. Leaflet.js mapping. Responsive design. GitHub template repository.
profile.operator: Open-source community project. Multiple contributors from public health informatics community.
profile.data_model: Configurable data input (CSV/JSON). Standard epidemiological visualizations. Customizable indicators and geographic boundaries.
profile.users_scale: Template used by dozens of organizations for COVID-19 and other disease dashboards. Community-maintained with contributions.
profile.access_model: Free and open-source. GitHub template. Fork and customize. MIT license. Documentation included.

all.d: Assessed DPRK biological weapons program - estimated 13 pathogen agents including anthrax, smallpox, plague; no verified surveillance platform

profile.overview: Assessed DPRK (North Korea) biological weapons program is an intelligence-assessed state biological weapons capability. North Korea is believed to maintain 13 types of biological agents that could be weaponized, though program details remain opaque.
profile.functional_scope: Assessed capabilities include: research on at least 13 biological agents (anthrax, plague, smallpox, cholera, botulinum toxin), potential weaponization and delivery, dual-use pharmaceutical production capacity, and limited defensive biodetection.
profile.tech_stack: Dual-use pharmaceutical and biotechnology infrastructure. Military biological research facilities. Limited international visibility into capabilities.
profile.operator: DPRK Ministry of National Defense and various military/civilian research institutions. No international oversight or transparency.
profile.data_model: Intelligence-assessed capability estimates. No confirmed data. Open-source analysis from defector testimony, satellite imagery, and diplomatic assessments.
profile.users_scale: Affects regional and global security calculations. Subject of US, ROK, Japanese, and international intelligence assessments.
profile.access_model: Intelligence-assessed program. No public access. Analysis available through academic papers, Congressional research reports, and nonproliferation organization publications.
